title: The evolutionary hijack
content:
What constitute true creativity is the openness and adaptability of our spirit. When we see or experience something we must be able to look at it from several angles, to see other possibilities beyond the obvious ones. 

Imagine that the objects around us can be used and coopted for different purposes. Do not hold on to our original idea out of sheer stubbornness, or because of ego.

Move with what presents itself to us in the moment, exploring and exploiting different branches and contingencies. 

The difference in our creative abilities lies in how we look at the world and the fluidity with which we can reframe what we see.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/The evolutionary hijack.md

title: Let go of the past - Adaption strategy
content:
You are not tied to a particular position; your loyalty is not to a career or a company. You are committed to your Life's Task, to giving it full expression. It is up to you to find it and guide it correctly. You are on your own. 

If change is forced upon you, you must resist the temptation to overreact or feel sorry for yourself. 

Remember: your life's task is a living, breathing organism. The moment you rigidly follow a plan set in your youth, you lock yourself into a position, and the times will ruthlessly pass you by.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Let go of the past - Adaption strategy.md

title: Dependency
content:
Do not carry the need of for approval from apprenticeship phase to creative phase. 

You ofc shall not ignore the judgments, but first must work hard to develop internal standards and a high degree of independence. When public reacts, you can distinguish what is worth paying attention to and what to ignore. Internalise the voice of you Master so that you become both teacher and pupil.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Dependency.md

title: Synthesize all forms of knowledge - the universal Man-Woman
content: Aspects of technology now offer unprecedented means to build connections between fields and ideas. The artificial barriers between the arts and the sciences will melt away under the pressure to know and to express our common reality. our ideas will become closer to nature, more alive and organic. In any way possible, you should strive to be a part of this universalising process, extending your own knowledge to other branches, further and further out. The rich ideas that will come from such a quest will be their own reward.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Synthesize all forms of knowledge - the universal Man-Woman.md

title: First principle about skills
content: Essential to begin with one skill that you can master, that serves as a foundation for acquiring others. Avoid idea that u can manage learning several skills at a time.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/First principle about skills.md

title: Find your way back - The life or death strategy
content: You must listen to the message of the frustration and pain and let it guide you. The way back from a wrong path to the right one requires sacrifice. You can't have everything in the present.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Find your way back - The life or death strategy.md

title: Second principle about skills
content: initial stages invariably involve tedium. Accept it and embrace it. Pain and boredome toughens our minds.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Second principle about skills.md

title: Internalise the details - the Life Force
content:
You must see whatever you produce as something that has a life and presence of its own. This presence can be vibrant and visceral, or weak and lifeless. 

Seeing your work as alive, your path to mastery is to study and absorb these details in a universal fashion, to the point at which you feel the life force and can express it effortlessly. in your work.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Internalise the details - the Life Force.md

title: Alternate the mind through "the current"
content:
The current is a constant dialogue between our thoughts and reality. If we go into this process deeply enough, we come into contact with a theory that explains something far beyond the capability of our limited senses.

The current is merely an intensification of the most elementary powers of human consciousness. 

Do not short circuit the current: do not observe a phenomenon that makes you emotional and run rampant with speculation. Take the time to entertain possible explanations. 

Do not accumulate huge amounts of info or data and never venture in speculations. Speculation is the heart and soul of human rationality, our way of connecting to reality and seeing the invisible. 

The response of the public will make you think more deeply about what you are producing. Such feedback will help make visible what is generally invisible to your eyes - the objective reality of your work and its flaws.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Alternate the mind through "the current".md

title: The open field
content:
A certain way of thinking or acting that once had success quickly becomes a paradigm, an established procedure. As the years go by, people forget the initial reason for this paradigm and simply follow a lifeless set of techniques. 

Begin by looking inward. You have something you want to express that is unique to yourself and related to your inclinations. Be sure it is not something that is sparked by some trend or fashion, but comes from you and is real. 
Let the idea, sound, image take root in you. Sensing the possibility of a new language, make the conscious decision to play against the very conventions that you find dead and want to get rid of. Following this strategy will give your work a kind of reverse reference point and a way to shape it. 

Bring to your new idea all of the knowledge you have acquired in your field, but for the purpose of reversing it. You are creating a space in a cluttered culture, claiming for yourself an open field in which you can finally plant something new.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/The open field.md

title: Conservatism
content:
Do not fall in love with the ideas and strategies that worked for you in the past. 

Do not become addicted to the material comforts you have acquired: ideas or beliefs that please audience, sponsor or whoever we want to please. 

Creativity is by its nature an act of boldness and rebellion.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Conservatism.md

title: Conformism
content:
Work-wise let your work speak for you. 

For matters as politics, morals, values, make a show of adhering to the accepted standards of your environment. Think of workplace as theater where you always wear a mask. Be careful to what you say.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Conformism.md

title: Occupy the perfect niche
content: You want to find a niche in the ecology job market that you can dominate. From the first chosen field you can look for side paths that particularly attract you. When possible, make a move to the narrower field. Continue until you eventually hit upon a totally unoccupied niche.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Occupy the perfect niche.md

title: Avoid the false path - Rebellion strategy
content:
Your strategy must be twofold:
##### 1
Realize as early as possible that you have chosen your career for the wrong reasons, before your confidence takes a hit.

##### 2
Actively rebel agains those forces that have pushed you away from your true path. Feel some anger at the parental forces that want to foist upon you an alien vocation. 
Let your sense of rebellion fill you with energy and purpose.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Avoid the false path - Rebellion strategy.md

title: First step to bring about serendipity
content:
Widen your search as far as possible. In research stage look at more than what is generally required. Expand research into other fields. 

Examine as many examples and counterexamples as humanly possible. What ensues is that the brain becomes increasingly excited and stimulated by the variety of information.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/First step to bring about serendipity.md

title: Value learning over money
content:
Your thoughts will tend to revolve around what you value most. 

Train yourself to get by with little money.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Value learning over money.md

title: Cultivate negative capability
content:
We must be capable of negating our ego. 

Truly creative people in all fields can temporarily suspend their ego and simply experience what they are seeing, without the need to assert a judgment, for as long as possible. They are more than ready to find their most cherished opinions contradicted by reality. 

This ability to endure and even embrace mysteries is negative capability. 

This quality allows you to entertain a broader range of ideas and experiment with them, which in turn makes their work richer and more inventive. 

In the sciences, you will tend to entertain ideas that fit your own preconceptions and that you want to believe in. This unconsciously colours your choices of how to very these ideas, and this is the confirmation bias. So you will find experiments and data that confirm what you have already come to believe in. 
The uncertainty of not knowing the answers beforehand is too much for most scientists. 

The need for certainty is the greatest disease the mind faces. 

Develop the habit of suspending the need to judge everything that crosses your path. Consider and even momentarily entertain pov opposite to your own, seeing how they feel. You observe a person or event for a length of time, deliberately holding yourself back from forming an opinion. Seek out what is unfamiliar - reading books from unfamiliar writers in unrelated fields. 

Do anything to break up your normal train of thinking and your sense that you already know the truth. 

To negate ego adopt humility toward knowledge. 

Negative capability should not be a permanent state of mind. Is a tool we use in the process to open the mind up temporarily to more possibilities. Once this way of thinking leads to a creative avenue of thought, we can give our ideas a clearer shape and gently let it go, returning to this attitude whenever we feel stale or blocked.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Cultivate negative capability.md

title: Advance through trial and error
content: You want to learn as many skills as possible, following the direction that circumstances lead you to,but only if they are related to your deepest interests. Value the process of self-discovery and making things that are of the highest quality. You avoid the trap of following one set career path. You are not sure where this will all lead, but you are taking full advantage of the openness of information, all of the knowledge about skills now at our disposal. You see that kind of work suits you and what you want to avoid at all cost. You move by trial and error.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Advance through trial and error.md

title: The high end
content:
Technical locks: in order to learn a subject or skill, we immerse in many details, techniques, and procedures that are standard for solving problems. If we are not careful, we become locked into seeing every problem in the same way, using the same techniques and strategies that became so imprinted in us. In the process, we lose sight of the bigger picture, the purpose of what we are doing, how each problem we face is different and requires a different approach. We adopt a tunnel vision. 

Your project/problem should always be connected to something larger - a bigger question, an inspiring goal. Whenever your work begins to feel stale, you must return to the larger purpose and goal that impelled you in the first place. This bigger idea governs your smaller paths of investigation, and opens up many more such paths for you to look into.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/The high end.md

title: Complacency
content:
As we pass the apprenticeship and begin to flex our creative muscles, we feel satisfaction in what we have learned and how far we have progressed. We naturally begin tot take for granted certain ideas we have learned and developed. We stop asking the same kinds of questions that plagued us earlier on, and give the answers to them for granted. 
The mind slowly narrows and tightens as complacency creeps into the soul. Doing so we stifle our creativity and never get it back. 

Fight this tendency by upholding the value of active wonder. Constantly remind yourself how little you truly know, and how mysterious everything else remain.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Complacency.md

title: Strategies for deepening the mentor dynamic
content:
[[Choose the mentor according to your need and inclinations]]

[[Gaze deep into the mentor's mirror]]

[[Transfigure their ideas]]

[[Create a back-and-forth dynamic]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Strategies for deepening the mentor dynamic.md
links: Choose the mentor according to your need and inclinations
Gaze deep into the mentor's mirror

title: Gaze deep into the mentor's mirror
content:
Master's are those who by nature have suffered to get where they are. They have experienced endless criticisms of their work, doubts about their progress, setbacks along the way. 

You must get the sharpest dose of reality that is possible from your mentor. 

Choose mentor who is known for supplying this form of tough love. If they shy away from giving it, force them to hold up the mirror that will reflect you as you are.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Gaze deep into the mentor's mirror.md

title: Step two - creative strategies
content:
The mind muscle naturally tightens up over time. Causes are two:
1. We rather to entertain the same thoughts and ways of thinking because they provide us with a sense of consistency and familiarity. Sticking with same methods saves us effort. 
2. When we work hard at a problem or idea, our minds naturally narrow their focus because of the strain and effort involved. This means that the further we progress on our creative task, the fewer alternative possibilities or viewpoints we tend to consider. 

[[Cultivate negative capability]]

[[Allow for serendipity]]

[[Alternate the mind through "the current"]]

[[Alter your perspective]]

[[Revert to primal forms of intelligence]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Step two - creative strategies.md
links: Revert to primal forms of intelligence

title: Play to your strength - supreme focus
content:
Key component in the path to mastery is determining your mental and psychological strengths and working with them.

Look deep within and come to an understanding of these particular strengths and weaknesses you possess, being as realistic as possible. Knowing them, you can lean on them with utmost intensity. You will not be burdened by conventions, not slowed down by having to deal with skills that go against your natural inclinations and strengths. In this way, your creative and intuitive powers will be naturally awakened. 

Achieving mastery in life often depends on those first steps that we take. It is not simply a question of knowing deeply our life task, but also of having a feel for our own ways of thinking and for perspectives that are unique to us.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Play to your strength - supreme focus.md

title: Natural powers
content:
Build into the creative process an initial period that is open ended. You give yourself time to dream and wander, to start out in a loose and unfocused manner. In this period, you allow the project to associate itself with certain powerful emotions, ones that naturally come out of you as you focus on your ideas. It is always easy to tighten up your ideas later on, and to make your project increasingly realistic and rational. 
But if you begin with a feeling of tightness and pressure, focusing on the funding, the competition, or people's opinions, you will stifle the associative powers of the brain and quickly turn the work into something without joy or life. 

Have wide knowledge of your field and others, giving your brain more possible associations and connections. 

Keep this process alive, never settle into complacency, as if your initial vision represents the endpoint. Cultivate profound dissatisfaction with your work and the need to constantly improve your ideas, along with a sense of uncertainty - you are not exactly sure where to go next, and this uncertainty drives the creative urge and keeps it fresh. Any kind of resistance or obstacle that crosses your path should be seen as yet another chance to improve your work.

Embrace slowness as a virtue in itself. When it comes to creative endeavours, time is always relative.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Natural powers.md

title: Reversal 3
content:
if you can't find a mentor you shall become your own teacher and mentor. You push yourself to learn from every possible source. You read more books than those who have formal education, developing this into a lifelong habit. 

As much as possible you try to apply your knowledge in some form of experiment or practice. Find for yourself second-degree mentors in the form of public figures who can serve as role models. Reading and reflecting on their experiences, you can gain some guidance.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Reversal 3.md

title: Keys to mastery 6
content:
The reasoning of masters is guided by intuition; their intuition springs from intense rational focus. The two are fused. 

It is not a matter of studying a subject for 20 years, and then emerging as a master. The time that leads to mastery is dependent on the intensity of our focus. The key to attaining this higher level of intelligence is to make our years of study qualitatively rich. Do not simply absorb information - internalise it and make it our own by finding some way to put this knowledge to practical use. Look for connection between various elements you are learning, hidden laws that you can perceive in the apprenticeship phase.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Keys to mastery 6.md

title: Confirming paradigms and ignoring anomalies
content:
In any fields there are paradigms - accepted ways of explaining reality. 

We routinely look for patterns confirming the paradigms and we ignore the anomalies or explain them away. But the anomalies themselves contain the richest information. 

They often reveal the flaws in our paradigms and open up new ways of looking at the world.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Confirming paradigms and ignoring anomalies.md

title: Allow for serendipity
content:
Brain constantly look for similarities, differences and relationships between what it processes. You must feed this natural inclination.

Let go of conscious control and allow chance to enter into the process. When we are consumed with a particular project,  our attention tends to become quite narrow as we focus deeply. We grow tense. In this state, our mind responds by trying to reduce the amount of stimuli we have to deal with. We close ourselves off from the world in order to concentrate on what is necessary. This can have the unintended consequence of making it harder for us to see other possibilities, to be more open and creative with our ideas.

When we are in a more relaxed state, our attention naturally broadens and we take in more stimuli. 
Discoveries happen also in moments of unstrained attention, when something unexpected enters the mental sphere and triggers a new fertile connection. Such chance associations and discoveries are known as serendipity - the occurrence of something we are not expecting.

[[First step to bring about serendipity]]

[[Second step to bring about serendipity]]

A reason serendipity plays large role in discoveries is that our minds are limited. We cannot explore all avenues and imagine every possibility. Random external stimuli lead us to associations we cannot come by our own. 

To help cultivate serendipity keep a notebook with you at all times. The moment any idea or observation comes, you note it down.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Allow for serendipity.md
links: First step to bring about serendipity
Second step to bring about serendipity

title: Trust the process
content:
Whenever we learn a skill, we frequently reach a point of frustration - what we are learning seems beyond our capabilities. Giving in to these feelings, we unconsciously quit on ourselves before we actually give up. The difference is not simply a matter of determination, but more of trust and faith. 

When it comes to mastering a skill, time is the magic ingredient. Assuming your practice proceeds at a steady level, over days and wees certain elements of the skill become hardwired. 

The frustration Is a sign of progress - a signal that your mind is processing complexity and requires more practice. Trusting this will all happen, you will allow the natural learning process to move forward and everything else will fall into place.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Trust the process.md

title: Impatience
content:
You tend to lose the energy you had when you were younger and hungrier. You will veer toward repetition - reusing same ideas and processes as a kind of shortcut. But the creative process requires continual intensity and vigor. 
Each exercise or project or problem is different. hurrying to the end or warming up old ideas will ensure a mediocre result. 

Ostinato rigore. 

To tackle impatience, cultivate pleasure in pain - come to enjoy the rigorous practice, pushing past your limits, and resisting the easy way out.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Impatience.md

title: Specific knowledge - reading people
content:
Ability to read people, to get a feel for how they see the world, and to understand their individuality. 

Pay less attention to the words that people say and greater attention to their tone of voice, the look in their eye, their body language. 

Cutting off your interior monologue and paying deep attention, you will pick up cues from them that will register with you as feelings or sensations. 

When you drop your defense mechanism and pay deep attention to others, you will need to lower your guard and open yourself up to their influence as well. But as long as your emotions and empathy are directed outward, you will be able to detach yourself when necessary and analyse what you have gleaned. Resiste the temptation to interpret what they say or do as somehow implicitly involving you - this will cause you to turn your thoughts inward and close off the immediacy of the connection.

As exercise: after you have known people for a while, try to imagine that you are experiencing the world from their point of view, placing yourself in their shoes and feeling what they feel. Look for any common emotional experiences - a trauma or difficulty you've had, for instance, that resembles in some way what they are going through. 

Take special note of how ppl respond to stressful situations. Be sensitive to any extreme behaviour on their part (EXTREME IN ANY WAY) Sometimes they wear this like a mask to hide the opposite, distract others from the truth. 

Avoid making judgments based on initial impressions. sometimes can be misleading. 

The more you can fathom about people's past and their way of thinking about things, the more deeply you can enter into their spirit. This way u will be able to understand their motivations, foresee their actions, and recognize how best to win them to your side. 

Keep in mind that ppl are changing, so do not have a static picture of them but continually update it.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Specific knowledge - reading people.md

title: See people as they are - social intelligence
content:
[[Thinking inside]]

[[Keys to mastery 4]]

[[Strategies for acquiring social intelligence]]

[[Reversal 4]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/See people as they are - social intelligence.md

title: Keys to mastery 2
content:
The apprenticeship must be conducted by each individual in his own way. To follow *precisely* the lead of others or advice from a book is self-defeating.

**Fundamental principle of apprenticeship**:
The goal of an apprenticeship is not money, a good position, a title, diploma, but rather the *transformation* of your mind and character. 

Choose a place of work and position that offer the greatest possibilities for learning.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Keys to mastery 2.md

title: The roots of masterly intuition
content:
Our high-level intuition has its roots firmly in the primitive versione. Intuition is driven by memory. When we take information of any kind, we store it in mnemonic networks in the brain. The stability and durability of these networks depends on repetition, intensity of experience, and how deeply we pay attention. 

instead of having to reason an answer through a step-by-step process, the answer come to consciousness with a feeling of immediacy. The extremely high number of experiences and networks that become hardwired allow the brains of Masters to explore an area that is so wide that it has the dimensions and feel of reality itself, of the dynamic. 

This intuitive form of intelligence was developed to help us process complex layers of information and gain a sense of the whole. 
The desire for what is simple and easy infects all of us. The only solution is to learn how to quiet the anxiety we feel whenever we are confronted with anything that seems complex or chaotic. 
In moments of perceived crisis, we must develop the habit of maintaining our cool and never overreacting. If situation is complex and others are reaching for simple black-and-white answers, or for conventional responses, we must make a point of resisting such a temptation.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/The roots of masterly intuition.md

title: Alchemical creativity and the unconscious
content:
Those who think in dualities - believing that there is such a thing as "real" and such a thing as "unreal", and that they are distinct entities that can never become blended into a third, alchemical element - are creatively limited, and their work can quickly become dead and predictable. 

To maintain a dualistic approach to life requires that we repress many observable truths, but in our unconscious and in our dreams we often let go of the need to create categories for everything, and are able to mix seemingly disparate and contradictory ideas and feelings together with ease. 

Actively explore the unconscious and contradictory parts of your personality, and to examine similar contradictions and tensions in the world at large. 
Expressing these tensions within your work in any medium will create a powerful effect on others, making them sense the unconscious truths or feelings that have been obscured or repressed.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Alchemical creativity and the unconscious.md

title: Awaken the dimensional mind - the creative active
content:
As you accumulate more skills and internalise the rules that govern your field, your mind will want to become more active, seeking to use this knowledge in ways that are more suited to your inclinations. What will impede this natural creative dynamic from flourishing is not lack of talent but your attitude. Feeling anxious and insecure, you will tend to turn conservative with your knowledge, preferring to fit into the group and sticking to the procedures you have learned. 
Instead, force yourself in the opposite direction. As you emerge from your apprenticeship, you must become increasingly bold. 

Instead of feeling complacent about what you know, expand your knowledge to related fields, giving your mind fuel to make new associations between different ideas. Experiment and look at problems from all possible angles. As your thinking grows more fluid your mind will become increasingly dimensiona, seeing more and more aspects of reality. In the end, you will turn against the very rules you have internalised, shaping and reforming them to suit your spirit. Such originality will bring you to the heights of power. 

[[Keys to mastery 5]]

[[Strategies for the creative-active phase]]

[[Reversal 5]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Awaken the dimensional mind - the creative active.md

title: Create a back-and-forth dynamic
content:
Mentors might be generationally different than us. At certain point, some of their principles might seem a bit out of touch or irrelevant, and we unconsciously tune them out. The only solution is to evolve a more interactive dynamic with the mentor. If they can adapt to some of your ideas, the relationship becomes more animated. Feeling a growing openness on their part to your input, you are less resentful. You are revealing to them your own experiences and ideas, perhaps loosening them up so their principles don't harden into dogma. 

With your intense focus, you improve in skill levels, giving you the power to introduce more of yourself and your needs. You give them feedback to their instruction, perhaps adjust some of their ideas. This must begin with you, as you set the tone with your hunger to learn.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Create a back-and-forth dynamic.md

title: General knowledge - The seven deadly realities
content:
Accumulating an understanding of the overall patterns of human behavior that transcend us as individuals.

Negative qualities: the seven deadly realities
- [[Envy]]
- [[Conformism]]
- [[Rigidity]]
- [[Self-obsessiveness]]
- [[Laziness]]
- [[Flightiness]]
- [[ Passive aggression]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/General knowledge - The seven deadly realities.md

title: Keys to mastery 3
content:
In the early stages of career you shall acquire practical knowledge in the most efficient manner possible. For this purpose, during the apprenticeship phase you will need mentors whose authority you recognize and to whom you submit. 

Almost all masters and people of power suffer from too many demands on their time and too much information to absorb. If you can demonstrate the ability to help them organise themselves on the fronts to a degree that others cannot, it will be much easier to get their attention and interest them in the relationship. Do not shy away from anything menial or secretarial. You want person-to-person access, however you can get it. 

Try to see the world through their eyes and ask the simple question of what it is they need most. 

One mentor at a time is best, but not always possible to find the perfect one. Then find severals in your immediate environment, each one filling strategic gaps in your knowledge and experience.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Keys to mastery 3.md

title: Look at the "what" instead of the "how"
content:
Focus on the structure of something instead of the single components. 
Think how the parts relate overall between each other. 

Pay more attention to the relationships between things, cos that will give you a greater feel for the picture as a whole.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Look at the "what" instead of the "how".md

title: Reversal 6
content:
The reversal to mastery is to deny its existence or its importance, and therefore the need to strive for it in any way. But such a reversal can only lead to feelings of powerlessness and disappointment. This reversal leads to enslavement to what we shall call the false self.

Your false self is the accumulation of all the voices you have internalized from other people—parents and friends who want you to conform to their ideas of what you should be like and what you should do, as well as societal pressures to adhere to certain values that can easily seduce you. It also includes the voice of your own ego, which constantly tries to protect you from unflattering truths. 

This self talks to you in clear words, and when it comes to mastery, it says things like, “Mastery is for the geniuses, the exceptionally talented, the freaks of nature. I was simply not born that way.” Or it says, “Mastery is ugly and immoral. It is for those who are ambitious and egotistical. Better to accept my lot in life and to work to help other people instead of enriching myself.” Or it might say, “Success is all luck. Those we call Masters are only people who were at the right place at the right time. I could easily be in their place if I had a lucky break.” Or it might also say, “To work for so long at something that requires so much pain and effort, why bother? Better to enjoy my short life and do what I can to get by.”

As you must know by now, these voices do not speak the truth. Mastery is not a question of genetics or luck, but of following your natural inclinations and the deep desire that stirs you from within. Everyone has such inclinations. This desire within you is not motivated by egotism or sheer ambition for power, both of which are emotions that get in the way of mastery. It is instead a deep expression of something natural, something that marked you at birth as unique. 

In following your inclinations and moving toward mastery, you make a great contribution to society, enriching it with discoveries and insights, and making the most of the diversity in nature and among human society. It is in fact the height of selfishness to merely consume what others create and to retreat into a shell of limited goals and immediate pleasures. Alienating yourself from your inclinations can only lead to pain and disappointment in the long run, and a sense that you have wasted something unique. This pain will be expressed in bitterness and envy, and you will not recognize the true source of your depression.

Your true self does not speak in words or banal phrases. Its voice comes from deep within you, from the substrata of your psyche, from something embedded physically within you. It emanates from your uniqueness, and it communicates through sensations and powerful desires that seem to transcend you. You cannot ultimately understand why you are drawn to certain activities or forms of knowledge. This cannot really be verbalized or explained. It is simply a fact of nature. In following this voice you realize your own potential, and satisfy your deepest longings to create and express your uniqueness. It exists for a purpose, and it is your Life’s Task to bring it to fruition.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Reversal 6.md

title: Strategies for finding your life's task
content:
The obstacles to overcome in this quest are the voices of others infecting you, fighting over limited resources, choosing false paths, getting stuck in the past and losing your way.

[[Return to your origins]]

[[Occupy the perfect niche]]

[[Avoid the false path - Rebellion strategy]]

[[Let go of the past - Adaption strategy]]

Let go of the past - Adaption strategy
Find your way back - The life or death strategy

title: Keep expanding your horizons
content:
You must struggle against any limitations and continually work to expand your horizons. In each learning situation you will submit to reality, but that reality does not mean you must stay in one place. 

Whenever you feel like you are settling into some circle, force yourself to shafe things up and look for new challenges.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Keep expanding your horizons.md

title: Apprentice yourself in failure
content:
Mistakes and failures are precisely your means of education. They tell you about your own inadequacies. Failures also permit you to see the flaws of your ideas, which are only revealed in the execution of them. 

There are two kinds of failure. The first comes from never trying out your ideas because you are afraid, or because you are waiting for the perfect time. This kind of failure you can never learn from, and such timidity will destroy you.
The second kind comes from a bold and venturesome spirit. If you fails in this way, the hit you take to your reputation is greatly outweighed by what you learn.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Apprentice yourself in failure.md

title: Choose the mentor according to your need and inclinations
content:
in selecting a mentor, you want to keep in mind your inclinations and life's task, the future position you envision for yourself. It should be strategically aligned with this. 

Sometimes part of what a mentor shows us is something we want to avoid or actively rebel against. In the latter case, you might initially want to maintain a little more emotional distance than normally recommended, particularly if she is the domineering type. Over time you will see that to absorb and what to reject. Remember that the mentor-dynamic replays something of the parental or father-figure dynamic.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Choose the mentor according to your need and inclinations.md

title: Suffer fools gladly
content:
Fools: when it comes to practical life, what should matter is getting long term results, and getting the work done in an efficient and creative a manner as possible. 
Fools carry with them a different scale of values. They place more importance on short-term matters. They are ruled by ego and insecurities. They enjoy drama and political intrigue for their own sake. When criticising, they emphasize matters that are irrelevant to the overall picture or argument. 

You can distinguish them by how little they get done, or by how hard they make it for others to get results. They lack a certain common sense, getting worked up about things that are not really important while ignoring problems that will spell doom in the long term.

Don't get to their level, when drawn by accusations and critiques. Rationality and results don't matter to them. You simply waste time and energy. 

All of us have foolish sides, momens in which we lose our heads and think more of our ego or short term goals. It is human nature. Seeing this foolishness within you, you can then accept it in others. This will allow you to smile at their antics, to tolerate their presence as you would a silly child, and to avoid the madness of trying to change them. 

The height of wisdom is to use them for material for your work, as examples of things to avoid, or by looking for ways to turn their actions to your advantage.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Suffer fools gladly.md

title: Combine the how and the what
content:
We live in two worlds. 
There is the outer world of appearences - all of the forms of things that captivate our eye. But hidden from our view is the world of how these things actually function, their anatomy or composition, the parts working together and forming the whole. This second world is not so immediately captivating. 

This "how" of things is just as poetic once we understand it - it contains the secret of life, of how things move and change. 

By combining the "how" and the "what" of a field, a subject, a domain, we gain a more rounded knowledge of the field. 

We must make ourselves study as deeply as possible the technology we use, the functioning of the group we work in, the economics of our field, its lifeblood. We must ask the question: How do things work? How do decisions get made? How does the group interact?
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Combine the how and the what.md

title: Connect to your environment - primal powers
content:
The ability to connect deeply to your environment is the most primal and in many ways the most powerful form of mastery the brain can bring us. 
We gain such power by first transforming ourselves into consummate observers. We see everything in our surrounding as a potential sign to interpret. Nothing is taken at face value.

To become sensitive observers we must not succumb to all of the distractions afforded by technology; must be a little primitive. Primary instrument is our eyes for observing and our brains for analysing. When we connect to the virtual environment, the power of our eyes and brain slowly atrophy. Must see your environment as a physical entity and your connection to it as visceral.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Connect to your environment - primal powers.md

title: The hidden force
content:
Leonardo didn't care much about the finished product; it was the search and process in creating something that had always excited him. 

'Just as a well-filled day brings blessed sleep, so a well-employed life brings a blessed death' Leonardo Da Vinci.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/The hidden force.md

title: The return to reality
content:
In our apprenticeship we begin by learning the parts and making various distinction - right and wrong way to proceed, individual skills to master and their particular techniques, various rules and conventions that govern the group.
In the creative-active we begin to melt these distinctions as we experiment with, shape, and alter these conventions to suit our purposes. 
And in mastery we come full circle, returning to a sense of the whole. We intuit and see the connections. We embrace the natural complexity of life, making the brain expand to the dimension of reality instead of shrinking it to the narrowest of specializations. 

The ultimate distinctions you make is between yourself and the world. There is the inside (your subjective experience) and there is the outside. But every time you lear something, your brain is altered as new connections are formed. Your experience of something that occurs in the world physically alters your brain. The boundaries between you and the world are much more fluid than you imagine.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/The return to reality.md

title: Widen your vision - the Global Perspective
content:
Most people are perpetually locked in the present. Their decisions are overly influenced by the most immediate event; they easily become emotional and ascribe greater significance to a problem than it should have in reality. 

Continually enlarge your perspective. Remind yourself of the overall purpose of the work you are engaged and how this meshes with long-term goals. 

Look at how it inevitably connects to a larger picture. 

Look wider and think further ahead.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Widen your vision - the Global Perspective.md

title: Reversal 5
content: To crate meaningful work of art or make a discovery requires great discipline, self-control, and emotional stability. It requires mastering the forms of your field. Drugs and madness only destroy such powers.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Reversal 5.md

title: Skills acquisition (the practice mode)
content:
Reduce the needed skills to something simple and essential, the core of what you need to get good at, skills that can be practiced. In acquiring any kind of skill, there exists a natural learning process that coincides with the functioning of our brains. This learning process leads to what we shall call tacit knowledge - a feeling for what you are doing that is hard to put into words but easy to demonstrate in action. 

This tacit knowledge comes from the incessant repetition of the practice of the tasks as the master do it. By imitating repeatedly only one can master a task, a profession, an art. Even more than learning through words or books, mirroring and imitating others over and over is the ultimate source of learning. 

Once you take this far enough, you enter a cycle of accelerated returns in which the practice becomes easier and more interesting, leading to the ability to practice for longer hours, which increases your skill level, which in turn makes practice even more interesting. Reaching this cycle is the goal you must set. To get there you must understand basic principles about skills:
- [[First principle about skills]]
- [[Second principle about skills]]

Once an action becomes automatic, you now have the mental space to observe yourself as you practice it. Use this distance to take note of your weaknesses or flaws that need correction - to analyse yourself. Also helps to get much feedback from others, to have standards against which you can measure your progress. 

Your skill is not something that can be put into words; it is embedded in your body and nervous system - it becomes tacit knowledge. 

When you practice and develop any skill you transform yourself in the process. You reveal to yourself the new capabilities that were previously latent, that are exposed as you progress. You develop emotionally. Your sense of pleasure becomes redefined. What offers immediate pleasures comes to seem like a distraction, and empty entertainment to help pass the time. 
Real pleasure comes from overcoming challenges, feeling confidence in your abilities, gaining fluency in skills, and experiencing the power this brings. You develop patience. Boredom no longer signals the need for distraction, but rather the need for new challenges to conquer.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Skills acquisition (the practice mode).md

title: Discover your calling - the life's task
content:
In childhood this force was clear to you. It directed you toward activities and subjects that fit your natural inclinations, sparked curiosity that was deep and primal.

title: Reversal
content:
When you are faced with deficiencies instead of strengths: ignore your weaknesses and resist the temptation to be more like others. Instead, direct yourself toward the small things you are good at. Do not dream or make grand plans for the future, but instead concentrate on becoming proficient at these simple and immediate skills. This will give you confidence and become a base from which you can expand to other pursuits. 

Your life's task can also appear in the guise of your deficiencies, making you focus on the one or two things that you are inevitably good at.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Reversal.md

title: Keys to mastery 4
content:
You must allow everyone the right to exist in accordance with the character he has, whatever it turns out to be: and all you should strive to do is to make use of this character in such a way as its kind of nature permits, rather than to hope for any alteration in it, or to condemn it offhand for what it is. This is the true sense of the maxim - live and let live. 

To become indignant at people's conduct is as foolish as to be angry with a stone because it rolls into your path. And with many people the wisest thing you can do, is to resolve to make use of those whom you cannot alter. 

As we grow we come to view our parent's actions through the lens of our needs, and so they become extensions of ourselves. 

The many years of viewing people through the lens of our emotional needs turn into a habit that we can hardly control. Let us call this the *Naive Perspective*.

Social intelligence is nothing more than the process of discarding the Naive Perspective and approaching something more realistic. It involves focusing our attention outward instead of inward, honing the observational and empathic skill that we naturally possess. It means moving past our tendency to idealize and demonize people, and seeing and accepting them as they are. 

You must avoid becoming cynical in your approach as an overreaction to your prior naiveté. Most effective attitude is one of supreme acceptance. 

You are an observer of the human comedy, and by being tolerant as possible, you gain a much greater ability to understand people and to influence their behaviour when necessary.

[[Specific knowledge - reading people]]

[[General knowledge - The seven deadly realities]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Keys to mastery 4.md
links: General knowledge - The seven deadly realities

title: The authentic voices
content:
The paradox is that those who impress the most with their individuality are the ones who first completely submerge their character in a long apprenticeship. 

The greatest impediment to creativity is your impatience, the almost inevitable desire to hurry up the process, express something, and make a splash. In this case you do not master the basics, you have no real vocabulary at disposal.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/The authentic voices.md

title: Step one - the creative task
content:
Begin by altering the very concept of creativity and by g to see it fro a new angle. 

Creative activity is one that involves the entire self - our emotions, our levels of energy, our characters, and our minds. 

tThe task that you choose to work on must have an obsessive elements. Like the life's task, it must connect to something within you. 

It is the choice of where to direct his or her creative energy that makes the master. 

Primary law of the creative dynamic: your emotional commitment to what you are doing will be translated directyl into your work. It ay be wise to choose something that appeals to your sense of unconventionality and calls up latent feeling of rebelliousness. In opting for something that has deep personal appeal to you, you will naturally move in an unorthodox direction. Try to ally this with a desire to subvert conventional paradigms and go against the grain. The sense of having enemies or doubters can serve as a powerful motivating device and fill you with an added creative energy and focus. 

Two things to keep in mind:
1. Task must be realistic. The knowledge and skills you have gained must be eminently suited to pulling it off. To reach your goal you may have to learn few new things, but you must have mastered the basics and possess a solid enough grasp of the field so that your mind can focus on higher matters. Always choose a task that is slightly above you. This is a corollary of the law of the creative dynamic - the higher the goal, the more energy you will call up from deep within. 
2. Let go of your need for comfort and security. Creative endeavours are by their nature uncertain. You may know your task but you are never exactly sure where your efforts will lead. If you are worried about failure or going through a period of mental and financial instability, then you will violate the primary law of the creative dynamic, and your worries will be reflected in the results. Think of yourself as an explorer.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Step one - the creative task.md

title: Submit to reality - The ideal apprenticeship
content:
[[Keys to mastery 2]]

[[The apprenticeship phase - the three steps or modes]]

[[Strategies for completing the ideal apprenticeship]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Submit to reality - The ideal apprenticeship.md
links: The apprenticeship phase - the three steps or modes
Strategies for completing the ideal apprenticeship

title: Emotional pitfalls
content:
[[Complacency]]

[[Conservatism]]

[[Dependency]]

[[Impatience]]

[[Grandiosity]]

[[Inflexibility]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Emotional pitfalls.md

title: Move toward resistance and pain
content:
Adopt resistance practice: go in the opposite direction of all of your natural tendencies when it comes to practice. 

First, resist the temptation to be nice to yourself. Become your own worst critic; you see your work as if through the eyes of others. 

You find a kind of perverse pleasure in moving past the pain this might bring. 

Second, you resist the lure of easing up on your focus. Train yourself to concentrate in practice with double the intensity.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Move toward resistance and pain.md

title: Flightiness
content:
We continually change our ideas and perceptions. Never assume that what people say or do in a particular moment is a statement of their permanent desires. 
Cultivate a degree of detachment from other ppl's shifting emotions. 
Focus on their actions, and less on their words.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Flightiness.md

title: Keys to mastery 5
content:
The mind of wonder that you have as a child, being surprised for things we now take for granted, let us call it the *Original Mind*.

masters and those with high level of creative energy are simply people who manage to retain a sizeable portion of their childhood spirit despite the pressure and demands of adulthood. 

We all possess an inborn creative force that wants to become active. This is the gift of our original mind, which reveals such potential. 

The dimensional mind has two essential requirements: one, a high level of knowledge about a field or subject; two, openness and flexibility to use this knowledge in new and original ways. 

The problem for us all is that the knowledge we gain in the apprenticeship phase -including rules and procedures - can slowly become a prison. It locks us into certain methods and forms a of thinking that are one-dimensional. The mind must be force from its conservative positions and made active and exploratory. 

To awaken the dimensional mind and move through the creative process requires three essential steps:
1. Choose the proper creative task, the kind of activity that will maximise our skills and knowledge
2. Loosening and opening up the mind through certain creative strategies
3. Creating the optimal mental conditions for breakthrough, or insight. 

Be aware of the emotional pitfalls - complacency, boredom, grandiosity, ecc.

[[Step one - the creative task]]

[[Step two - creative strategies]]

[[Step three - the creative breakthrough - tension and insight]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Keys to mastery 5.md
links: Step three - the creative breakthrough - tension and insight

title: Deep observation (the passive mode)
content:
Task upon entering the work world firstly is to observe and absorbe its reality as deeply as possible. Do not try hard to get attention, impress people, and prove yourself. 

If you impress people, it should be because of the seriousness of your desire to learm, not because you are trying to rise to the top before you are ready. 

Pay lot of attention to both the "written rules" of 'this is how we do it here' but also to the unstated rules that are part of the underlying work culture. These concern style and values that are considered important. They are often a reflection of the character of the man or woman on top. 

You should be like an anthropologist, you are not there to change the culture, at least for this moment in time. 

As you amass more information about the rules and power dynamic of new environment, you can begin to analyse why they exist, and how they relato to larger trends in the field. You move from observation to analysis, honing your reasoning skills, but only after months of careful attention. 

Submit to and absorb the reality of all aspects of your voyage.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Deep observation (the passive mode).md

title: Thinking inside
content:
The principal problem we face in the social arena is our tendency to project onto people our emotional needs and desires of the moment. We misread their intentions and react in ways that cause confusion or conflict. Social intelligence is the ability to see people in the most realistic light possible. BY moving past our usual self-absorption, we can learn to focus deeply on others, reading their behavior in the moment, seeing what motivates them, and discerning any possible manipulative tendencies. 

Take step backward and don't get emotional. Focus completely on the people u dealing with, cutting off your own insecurities and desires from the equation. 
Accept completely and radically the condition of human nature. 

It then becomes all manoeuvres on a chessboard, and by thinking inside the other person you are able to play the game to perfection.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Thinking inside.md

title: Revert to primal forms of intelligence
content:
Human working memory is limited. Through image we can simultaneously imagine many things at one, at a glance. Use of images to make sense of the world is the most primitive form of intelligence, and can help us conjure up ideas that we can later verbalise. 

Using diagrams and models to help further the creative process can be immensely productive. 

Creative people do not simply think in words, but use all of their sense, their entire bodies in the process. They find sense cues that stimulate their thoughts on many levels. They are more open to alternative ways of thinking, creating, and sensing the world. They allow themselves a broader range of sense experience. 

You shall also expand your notion of thinking and creativity beyond the confines of words and intellectualisations. Stimulating your brain and senses from all directions will help unlock your natural creativity and help revive your original mind.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Revert to primal forms of intelligence.md

title: Speak through your work
content:
Your work is the single greatest means at your disposal for expressing your social intelligence. By being efficient and detail oriented in what you do, you demonstrate that you are thinking of the group at large and advancing its cause. 

By making what you write clear and easy to follow, you show your care for the audience or public at large. 

By involving other people in your projects and gracefully accepting their feedback, you reveal your comfort with the group dynamic.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Speak through your work.md

title: Submit to the other - the Inside Out Perspective
content:
We can never really experience what other people are experiencing. We always remain on the outside looking in, and this is the cause of so many misunderstandings and conflicts. But the primal source of human intelligence comes from the development of mirror neurons, which gives us the ability to place ourselves in the skin of another and imagine their experience. Through continual exposure to people, and by attempting to think inside them, we can gain an increasing sense of the perspective, but these requires effort on our part.
Our natural tendency is to project and other people our own beliefs and values systems, in which way we are not even aware. 

'As soon as you feel yourself against me you have ceased to understand my position and consequently my arguments. You have to be the victim of the same passion'
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Submit to the other - the Inside Out Perspective.md

title: Second step to bring about serendipity
content:
Maintain openness and looseness of spirit. In moments of great tension and searching, you allow yourself moments of release. 
Take walks, engage in activities outside your work, think about something else no matter trivial. 
When some new and unanticipated idea now enters your mind, you do not ignore it cos it is irrational or does not fit the narrow frame of your previous work. You give it instead full attention and explore where it leads you.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Second step to bring about serendipity.md

title: Keys to mastery 1
content:
First you must reconnect with your inclinations, that sense of uniqueness. The first step is always inward. You search the past for signs of that inner voice or force. You look for an underlying pattern, a core to your character that you must understand as deeply as possible. 

Second, look at the career path you are about to being. The choice of it - or redirection - is critical. To help in this stage you will need to enlarge your concept of work itself. Your work then is something connected deeply to who you are, not a separate compartment in your life. You develop then a sense of your vocation. 

Begin by choosing a field that roughly corresponds to your inclinations. This offers you room to manoeuvre and important skills to learn. Not too ambitious, not too lofty. Continue to learn and experience as much as you can. Eventually you'll hit upon a particular field that suits you perfectly. You recognize it cos it will spark the childlike sense of wonder and excitement. It will feel right. 

What we lack most in the modern world is a sense of a larger purpose to our lives. 

Pindaro 'Become who you are by learning who you are'.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Keys to mastery 1.md

title: Step three - the creative breakthrough - tension and insight
content:
If we remained as excited as we were in the beginning of our project, maintaining that intuitive feel that sparked it all, we would never be able to take the necessary distance to look at our work objectively and improve upon it. Losing that initial verve causes us to work and rework the idea. It forces us to not settle too early on an easy solution. The mounting frustration and tightness that comes from single minded devotion to one problem or idea will naturally lead to a breaking point. 

We realize we are getting nowhere. Such moments are signals from the brain to let go, for however long a period necessary. 
When we let go, we are not aware that below the surface of consciousness the ideas and the associations we had built up continue to bubble and incubate. With the feeling of tightness gone, the brain can momentarily return to that initial feeling of excitements and aliveness, which by now has been greatly enhanced by all of our hard work. Brain can now find the proper synthesis to the work. 

Be aware of this process and encourage yourself to go as far as you can with your doubts, reworkings, strained efforts, knowing the value and purpose of the frustration and creative blocks you are facing. 

The feeling that we have endless time to complete our work has an insidious and debilitating effect on our minds. 
Working with deadlines, real or manufactured, always helps. 

[[Emotional pitfalls]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Step three - the creative breakthrough - tension and insight.md

title: Reversal 4
content: If you have not the patience required for managinf and mastering the more subtle and manipulative sides of human nature, then keep yourself away from thos situations as best possible. This will rule out working in groups larger than a handful of people.
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Reversal 4.md

title: Strategies for attaining mastery
content:
Masters have a strong inner guiding system and a high level of self-awareness. What has suited others in the past does not suit them. And so as they progress, they make a choice at a key moment of their lives: they decide to forge their own route, one that others will see as unconventional, but that suits their own spirit and rhythms and leads them closer to discovering the hidden truths of their objects of study. This key choice takes self-confidence and self-awareness. 

[[Connect to your environment - primal powers]]

[[Play to your strength - supreme focus]]

[[Internalise the details - the Life Force]]

[[Widen your vision - the Global Perspective]]

[[Submit to the other - the Inside Out Perspective]]

[[Synthesize all forms of knowledge - the universal Man-Woman]]
file_path: Self-Help/Mastery/Strategies for attaining mastery.md
