title: Atomic habits notes
content:
==**1% improvement**==
It is tiny improvement (unnoticeable at first) which creates incredible change. 
Success is the product of daily habits, not once in a lifetime transformation.

**==The truth about progress==**
Linear progress is what we expect but we actually experience "exponential progress". The time for the exponential progress to cross the linear progress is called the valley of disappointment. There are little results and most people fall back into old routine. Patience is required.

**==Goals vs Systems==**
Goals are good for setting a direction but systems are best for making progress. 

**==3 layers of behaviour change==**
Outcomes: what you get.
Process: what you do, routine and daily habits.
Identity: what you believe, how you think about yourself.
While most focus is given to outcomes, you should place much emphasis on identity. Start focusing on the person you want to become (goal is not to learn to trade, is to become a trader)
When you workout, you are an athlete. When you give instructions, you are a leader ...

**==The habit loop==**
Cue (phone ring) --> Craving (want to know who messaged) --> Response (pick up the phone) --> Reward (solve the problem of who messaged)
Over time reward become associated with cue. The more you repeat these habit loops, the stronger they become. 
How to influence the habit loops to work for us?

*The 4 Laws*

**==Law 1: Make It Obvious==**
Habit scorecard: write down any daily habit (ANY). Categorise as each habit as positive negative or neutral (based on the person you want to be)
[[Quotes]] : "until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate"
Good way to get habit started: I will [action] at [time] in [location]
You need to clearly state your intention. 

**==Stacking habits==**
Tie a desired habit to an existing one. Use the formula: I will [new habit] after [current habit]. Chain of habits is more likely to be sustained. You need a trigger cue, something you do automatically (brushing teeths for eg)

**==Law 2: Make it attractive==**
Trick your dopamine to chain a new habit with a reward after it (eg: dark chocolate after number of pages ridden)

**==Make bad habits unattractive==**
Highlights the negative consequences that bad habits have. Repeat them in your mind and visualise them as neatly as possible. Create as more friction as possible with those habits.  

**==Law 3: Make it easy==**
Framing habits formation in terms of time is flawed. It should be framed in terms of number of repetitions. 
Habits are more likely to occur when they require less energy. The bigger the obstacle, the greater the friction. Reduce the friction wherever possible. Starting easy can be a way. 

**==The 2 minute rule==**
Find a simle 2 minute versino of the desired habit, scale down the desired outcome (reading an hour becomes reading one page, meditating 1h becomes sitting on the ground). Once you'll find yourself in that situation though (sit on the ground), meditation will start more naturally as you've already made it that far. And it's ok if at the beginning it's only little. Then from there on progress with difficulty. 

**==Law 4: Make it satisfying==**
Keep a counter for the habit (piling up coins, moving forward paper clips ...), you need to reward yourself and feel successful after that.
Use habit tracker (Notion), crossing habits checks may be satisfying/recurring. When you read 30 min check it on Notion and cross it.  

**==Don't break the chain==**
Do no miss on your habits. If you miss for even 2 days, that is the start of a bad habit. Even if it can be a bad day, still do it little (only 10pushups, only 30 min of meditation, only 15 min of reading)

**==Habit contract==**
Punish yourself if you do not fulfill an habit, real punishment, something inconvenient and annoying. Stick to it and make it stand out if you miss out the habit.

title: Ikigai
content:
Your ikigai refers to a union of the things you love, what the world needs in your generation, what you get paid to do, and what you're good at.


Your mind will decay if you allow it to remain inactive. Scientifically, some vital neural connections will deteriorate if you don't engage them through mental exercises.


Some mental exercises to avoid being idle include:
• Learning a skill
• Playing board games
• Taking a dance class
• Teaching


Another thing that ages people too quickly is excessive stress. Having conducted several studies, the American Institute of Stress has attributed most health problems to stress and how it inhibits the activity of certain vital hormones that fight heart disease, depression, and anxiety. 

  

Some simple activities to incorporate into your daily life will help boost your immune system and make you healthier:
• Walk to work or take a twenty-minute walk each day.
• Take the stairs instead of the elevator.
• Spend your time socializing or taking part in leisure activities.
• Embrace healthy eating habits.
• Make sure you get enough sleep.
• Be intentional about your daily routines. Replace negative patterns with positive ones.


The meaning of life is to embrace finding your purpose and taking steps toward it. Anything else you do will lead to frustration. 

  
**Discovering your ikigai begins with identifying when you are happiest and what makes you forget your worries.** 

  

Several strategies can help you achieve flow more easily.
Strategy 1: Choose a task with a considerable level of complexity.
Strategy 2: Be clear about the goal you want to achieve.
Strategy 3: Focus on the task at hand.
Don't try to multitask. Choose one activity and pour your all into doing it well.

  
The following are vital conclusions from the book:
• They eat lots of vegetables.
• At least five meals a day contain fruit and other greens.
• Their diet fundamentally has grain.
• The only sugar they consume is cane sugar.
• Their salt intake is very low, half of what those in other Japanese areas consume.
• Their calorie consumption is low.


Here are some foods that Okinawans eat to stay healthy and live long: tofu, carrots, goya (bitter melon), cabbage, nori (seaweed), onion, soy sprouts, soybeans (boiled or raw), sweet potato, peppers, sanpincha (jasmine tea)

  
Change the things you can, but do not allow the things you can’t change to change you.

title: Ch by Ch summary - AGSP
content:
### Part 1 – Readiness

#### • Chapter 1 – Soul: Motivate Yourself

– Why it matters: show the gap between everyday “you” and the nobler “soul” you hope to become 

##### Key points

- **Soul Detector tool** – list past noble acts, eccentric desires, and true needs to sketch your personal virtue profile 
    
- The noble soul’s traits (just, courageous, restrained, magnanimous, liberal, prudent, wise) give you a built‑in moral compass 
    
- Self‑persuasion works because your soul is an ever‑present audience  
    – Notable quote: “Wish is a form of appetite…” — Aristotle
    

#### • Chapter 2 – Kairos: Pluck the Day

– Purpose: train yourself to see moments of chaos or change as invitations to act 

##### Key points

- **Kairotic moment** = the right‑time/right‑place opening; crises (a fall on the ice, a lay‑off) often create it
    
- **Gap‑mapping & creative clearing** – map the space between current state and desired one, then look for timing cues
    
- Think in **future‑tense (deliberative) rhetoric** to regain agency over decisions   
    – Example: author’s broken leg reroutes his New Year plans, illustrating chance‑as‑opportunity
    

#### • Chapter 3 – Inspiration: Find Illumination

– Purpose: show where ideas come from and how to capture them fast

##### Key points

- Four spark sources: muses (art), molecules (brain chemistry), movement, and mingling
    
- **Idea‑notebook** habit plus “compounding”: review sparks daily so small notes build into big insights 
    
- Treat boredom walks or showers as “creative clearings” where insights surface
    

#### • Chapter 4 – Hyperbole: Blow Things Up

– Purpose: set one daring, slightly impossible goal to jolt yourself into motion 

##### Key points

- Capital‑H **Hyperbole** = bold feat just beyond the possible; risk + drama make it motivating
    
- Requires full rhetorical toolkit (ethos, pathos, logos) to sustain it
    
- Example: author’s “Fifty Woos” luxury trip to romance his wife and test self‑persuasion 
    

---

### Part 2 – Grasp

#### • Chapter 5 – Ethos: Trust Yourself

– Purpose: build self‑credibility so your inner audience believes you 

##### Key points

- Project **beneficence** (goodwill), **rectitude** (virtue), **deftness** (skill) toward yourself
    
- Ben Franklin’s 13‑virtue chart shows experimental ethos‑building
    
- “Fake it, then earn it” – act the ideal; habits will back‑fill character
    

#### • Chapter 6 – Pathos: Control Your Mood

– Purpose: use feelings as fuel rather than obstacles

##### Key points

- Catalog inner emotions (confidence, shame, fear, envy, humor) and learn triggers 
    
- **Desire‑reversal drill**: flip cravings into reminders of long‑term aims
    
- Humor and music offer quick mood resets (e.g., heartbreak songs for catharsis)
    

#### • Chapter 7 – Logos: Come to Believe

– Purpose: give your self‑talk clear reasoning structures 

##### Key points

- **Induction via Four Causes** (material, formal, efficient, final) for big goals
    
- **Enthymeme** (2‑step deduction): proof → conclusion to reinforce confidence (“I learn fast, so I can do this job”)
    
- Logos pairs with **framing** to make hard choices feel inevitable
    

#### • Chapter 8 – Framing: Define Your Life

– Purpose: show how word‑choice reshapes reality

##### Key points

- **Broaden / redefine / focus**—three moves to tilt any inner debate 
    
- Spot fallacies like the **unit fallacy** to avoid self‑deception
    
- Renaming chores (“laundry” → “future‑me gratitude”) improves follow‑through
    

---

### Part 3 – Action

#### • Chapter 9 – Habit: Adjust Your Routine

– Purpose: make change automatic through tiny, stacked behaviors 

##### Key points

- **Lure & Ramp** – picture a glorious end‑state, then cut effort into micro‑chunks 
    
- **Tortoise Method** – slow ratchet: lock one habit before adding the next 
    
- Create a **personal time zone** (unused hour) for new practices
    

#### • Chapter 10 – Charms: Change the Voice in Your Head

– Purpose: craft short rhythmic lines that overwrite doubt 

##### Key points

- **Paean rhythm**—“Just one foot on the floor, then stand up!”—links sound to action 
    
- Repetition strengthens neural pathways; slogans literally re‑wire belief 
    
- Mix figures (alliteration, antithesis) and tropes (metaphor, synecdoche) for extra stickiness
    

#### • Chapter 11 – Narrative: Tell Your Story

– Purpose: turn your goals into a plot that makes success feel fated 

##### Key points

- **Connections > events** – tie (problem) & untie (response) sequences reveal the soul 
    
- Five “Save the Cat” foundation beats (Catalyst, Break‑into‑Two, Midpoint, All‑Is‑Lost, Break‑into‑Three) give any change arc 
    
- Motto, deadline, and flaw list (“Six Things That Need Fixing”) add drama
    

#### • Chapter 12 – Experimentation: Test the Tools

– Purpose: run live trials, measure, iterate 

##### Key points

- Design playful experiments that bundle the whole toolbox (author’s birthday mountain run)
    
- Track metrics (time, reps, pages) to convert abstract goals into data
    
- View setbacks as data points, not failures
    

---

### Part 4 – Solution

#### • Chapter 13 – Joy: Find Happiness

– Purpose: show how aligned habits and soul produce durable joy 

##### Key points

- **Tetrapharmakos** – four‑part “medicine” against fear (divine wrath, death, pain, money anxiety) 
    
- Practice moderate appetites; separate “needs” from “wants”
    
- **Thaumaston** – cultivate moments of awe (Double Rainbow effect) as proof of alignment
    

#### • Chapter 14 – Peroration: Tap the Power of Words

– Purpose: pull everything together and call for lifelong soul‑bending 

##### Key points

- Rhetoric is humanity’s core skill; mastering it for self‑change lets you help others
    
- Purpose isn’t found, it’s **activated** when daily self meets noble soul 
    
- Final charge: keep experimenting with words, then spread the craft
    

---

### Major Themes & Methods Not Yet Mentioned

- **Psychagogia** – leading (or bending) the soul through language underlies every chapter.
    
- **Chaos‑as‑signal** – continual reminder to treat disruptions as rhetorical prompts for change.
    
- **Figure/Trope taxonomy** – dozens of named devices (paean, metonymy, synecdoche) double as mindset tools.
    
- **Neuro‑rhetoric** – repetition activates the ventromedial prefrontal cortex, hard‑coding new beliefs .
    
- **Ethical safeguard** – author warns that the same tools can manipulate others; self‑use builds character first.
    

---

### Concluding Takeaway

By mapping your soul, seizing opportune moments, reasoning clearly, naming realities, routinising action, chanting purposeful words, storytelling your progress, and testing boldly, you can **persuade the one audience that truly matters—yourself—and, in doing so, approach Aristotle’s vision of a happy, fully human life.**
file_path: Self-Help/Aristotle's Guide to Self-Persuasion/Ch by Ch summary - AGSP.md

title: AGSP
content:
#### What the book is about

- **Big idea:** You can use the same tricks ancient speakers used to sway crowds—but point them at yourself.
    
- **Goal:** Change your own habits and feelings so you end up happier and more purposeful.
    

---

#### How the book is built

|Part|What it’s really saying|
|---|---|
|**1. Get Ready**|Figure out who you want to be and spot the right moment to start.|
|**2. Learn the Tools**|Pick up four classic persuasion moves (ethos, pathos, logos, framing).|
|**3. Take Action**|Turn those moves into daily routines, little pep‑talks, and small experiments.|
|**4. Lock It In**|Enjoy the results and keep them going for the long run.|

---

### Part 1 – Get Ready

1. **Know your “better self.”**
    
    - List times you were brave, kind, fair, etc.
        
    - Notice gaps between that ideal version and everyday you.
        
2. **Watch for openings.**
    
    - Chaos or change often creates chances to start fresh (new job, breakup, move).
        
3. **Collect sparks.**
    
    - Keep a pocket notebook or phone note. Jot any idea that excites you.
        
4. **Set one bold, slightly crazy goal.**
    
    - Example: run a 10 k if you’ve never jogged. The stretch wakes you up.
        

---

### Part 2 – Learn the Tools

1. **Ethos (character).**
    
    - Act in ways that make you trust yourself: be helpful, keep promises, get better at a skill.
        
2. **Pathos (emotion).**
    
    - Use feelings (hope, humor, even a little healthy shame) to push yourself forward.
        
3. **Logos (reason).**
    
    - Talk to yourself with clear reasons: “If I save €5 a day, I’ll have €1,800 in a year.”
        
4. **Framing (word choice).**
    
    - Label chores in a way that sounds fun or meaningful. “Laundry” → “Future‑me gratitude.”
        

---

### Part 3 – Take Action

1. **Build tiny habits.**
    
    - Slice big goals into steps so easy you can’t say no—like one push‑up a day.
        
2. **Create quick “charms.”**
    
    - Short lines you tell yourself when tempted: “Not now, that’s tomorrow‑me’s snack.”
        
3. **Tell a new story.**
    
    - See yourself as the hero of a bigger plot (“I’m the friend who always shows up on time”).
        
4. **Run experiments.**
    
    - Try a 7‑day test, measure, adjust, repeat.
        

---

### Part 4 – Lock It In

- **Joy check:** When daily actions match your “better self,” you feel lighter and more satisfied.
    
- **Keep speaking:** The stronger your inner voice gets, the easier it is to help others too.
    

---

### How everything links together

1. **Know yourself** →
    
2. **Spot the right moment** →
    
3. **Use tools to convince yourself** →
    
4. **Turn that conviction into habits** →
    
5. **Habits rewrite your story** →
    
6. **Story brings lasting happiness.**
    

---

### The takeaway in one line

Use ancient rhetoric on the one person who’s always listening—**you**—and your life will shift in the direction you choose.

[[Ch by Ch summary - AGSP]]

title: Io sono io
content:
Formulazione del proprio acrostico.

1. Non utilizzare parole che esprimono come ci piacerebbe apparire o raccontarci. 
2. Non utilizzare parole derivanti da giudizi ripetutamente ricevuti nel corso della propria vita.
3. Non temere di esprimere concetti contraddittori.
4. Non cedere alla falsa modestia.
5. Non pensare a ciò che sarebbe bello o giusto, agli interpreti che ci piacerebbe avere o ad aspetti che, intellettualmente, condividiamo; concentrati solo su ciò che senti ti appartiene. 

Le parole del nostro acrostico non rappresentano il nostro essere, rappresentano semmai gli interpreti del nostro essere, I nostri interpreti del sé.
Attenzione: il nostro essere contiene la sinfonia, agli interpreti è dato solo il compito di eseguire le diverse partiture. 

==Pratica==
Scrivi il tuo acrostico seguendo le indicazioni sopra. Non preoccuparti se di qualche lettera non trovi subito una parola soddisfacente. La troverai. O meglio, lei troverà te. Se il tuo nome è breve fallo anche del cognome

F --> Furbo
R --> Rigoroso
A --> Acerbo/Anomalo
N --> Narcisista
C --> Carismatico
E --> Egocentrico/Eccentrico
S --> Sarcastico
C --> Cordiale
O --> Organizzativo

title: Non siamo I nostri limiti
content:
Il discrimine fra gli umani non è fra chi ha talento e chi no; neppure fra chi è consapevole del proprio talento e chi no. Il vero discrimine è fra chi lo ricerca e chi no: si può morire ignari del proprio talento, ma non si può vivere autenticamente senza ricercarlo. 

Fiducia nelle persone significa innanzitutto fiducia nelle loro possibilità e nei loro talenti, nei talenti di ciascuno. Il valore della fiducia rappresenta pertanto uno dei prerequisiti della cultura del talento. La cultura del talento vuole che ogni persona possa esprimere almeno un talento. Può non saperlo, non vederlo, ignorarlo, ma ce l'ha. 

Nel talento risiede l'identità degli individui.

==Pratica==
Pensa a caratteristica de tuo partner che vedi come difetto. Guarda a quella caratteristica con sguardo benevolo. Ora scrivi quel difetto e indica tre motivi per cui quella caratteristica può essere positiva per la vostra relazione. 
Fai uguale per I tuoi genitori, per il tuo capo e per un tuo amico.

- Dima non si sa organizzare in maniera responsabile ed efficiente con lo studio. 
- Grazie a ciò posso esserle più vicina per quanto riguarda lo studio, l'organizzazione e la motivazione, cercando di spronarla e dandole stimoli
- Quando condivide con me questa debolezza mi sento più vicino e intimo a lei, conoscendo e imparando questo suo punto debole
-

title: Nuova percezione di sé
content:
Per procedere verso una più autentica percezione di noi stessi occorre dunque prendere coscienza che ogni nostro interprete del sé possiede almeno un talento: non esistono interpreti privi di talento.

La consapevolezza della propria multiformità, e della bellezza che essa contiene, ci può trasmettere anche una nuova percezione della solitudine: in fondo non siamo mai davvero soli, siamo sempre in compagnia dei nostri interpreti. 
Questa nuova percezione ci aiuta ad accettare il senso di solitudine a volte può attanagliarci e rifuggire dalla tentazione di ricercare nelle mode, nel "rumore", nell' iperattività e nelle dipendenze, una facile ma vana fuga. 

==Pratica==
Descrivi in terza persona il personaggio indicato da ciascuna parola del tuo acrostico. Fallo con stesso spirito con cui un genitore descrive I figlioli. 
Per ciascuno di loro, indica almeno un talento. Ricorda l'insegnamento: il talento può essere contenuto anche in una caratteristica apparentemente debole.

title: Autenticità e verità
content:
Il concetto di autenticità non sempre deve andare di pari passo con il concetto di verità.

E se dal nostro dire la verità derivassero grandi sofferenze?

Questo è un paradosso: a volte è molto meno autentico dire la verità per il proprio bisogno di dimostrare amicizia/trasparenza. 

Una falsa concezione della sincerità che porta alla svalutazione di sè per autoassegnarsi il diritto di svalutare gli altri in un esercizio di frustrante negazione delle possibilità di affermazione dell'individuo

==Pratica==
Scrivi il nome del tuo partner ed elenca 5 bisogni nei suoi confronti e 5 desideri per lei.
Dima
- Ispirazione e stimolo per migliorarmi sempre
- Equilibrio nello studio e vita
- Senso di leggerezza e spensieratezza
- Nuovi interessi e passioni
- Grande intimità

- Maggior determinazione per lo studio
- Migliore organizzazione per studio
- Trovare posto ad amsterdam
- Passioni extra scuola/lavorative
- Complicità con un ragazzo


Fai altrettanto per il tuo migliore amico.

title: Accettare se stessi
content:
I personaggi che interpretiamo nel tempo possono cambiare, ma ciò che siamo veramente tende a rimanere stabile. 
Farcene una ragione ci aiuta a vivere in armonia con noi stessi e ad accettarci per ciò che effettivamente e contradditoriamente siamo, consapevoli che in ogni nostro vizio si cela una virtù e viceversa.

Per esprimere compiutamente se stessi occorra accettare la propria multiformità e contraddittorietà.
Anche I vizi (capitali) sono ricchi di multiformità  e contraddittorietà.
Addirittura l'invidia contiene il germe dell'ambizione, del miglioramento, dell' avanzamento, della sfida. 

L'allineamento con l'identità conferisce energia al nostro navigare, giacchè il senso di stanchezza che a volte proviamo non deriva tanto dai km che ci lasciamo alle spalle, quanto dalla nebbia che vediamo di fronte a noi.

L'allineamento con la propria identità non può prescindere da una constatazione che deve diventare un profondo convincimento: ogni individuo è multiforme e contraddittorio. 

Non affezionarti a una parte di te o di un'altra persona che viene fuori solo in un certo contesto. Noi non siamo solo quel contesto, siamo molto altro. È possibile che in altri contesti emergano altre parti di noi che siano più o meno gradevoli. 

Ogni individuo è migliore di quanto possa apparire. 

Non esiste ruolo o atteggiamento che meriti una piena e totalizzante identificazione, neppure il ruolo di genitore, poichè nell'identificazione con un ruolo è spesso contenuta la paura verso le responsabilità derivanti dall'esercizio di quel ruolo. 

Il distacco dall'identificazione col ruolo libera energie e opportunità e ci mette in contatto con la nostra libertà individuale, fatta di possibili scelte. 

Dunque nessuno è la cosa che fa, ognuno è sicuramente e inevitabilmente molto di più.
Non siamo neppure la sommatoria di ciò che facciamo e degli atteggiamenti che assumiamo. C'è qualcos'altro che concorre a determinare la nostra identità e unicità. Chiamala coscienza, volontà, libero arbitrio. È il comandante della nostra nave personale. Quella parte di noi che a volte lascia spazio alle altre parti, ma sa. Anzi, sente. Quella parte di noi che è benevolmente consapevole della nostra multiformità. Quella parte di noi che si fa sentire e che chiamiamo voce interiore, l'inner monologue.

Spesso si etichetta come relativismo etico l'orientamento a includere le diversità, mettendo in discussione le proprie credenze. Apprendere dalla diversità non è relativismo etico, è, semmai, coraggio della scoperta. 

==Pratica==
Scrivi un elenco di tutti I ruoli che eserciti. Evidenzia quelli che tendi a "portarti dietro", anche in contesti in cui quel ruolo non è adeguato.
- Businessman anche a casa

Scrivi elenco di atteggiamenti tipici. Segnala quelli che tendi a replicare con frequenza eccessiva e che, dopo averli assunti, ti fanno provare un senso di inadeguatezza, disagio o colpa e ti procurano un sofferto dialogo interno. 
- 

Scrivi 3 cose che ti riescono veramente bene, anche piccole e apparentemente banali. 
- navigare su maps e earth
- 

Scrivi una parola nella quale, più di altre, ti identifichi.

Elenca I gruppi ai quali appartieni osservando le raccomandazioni contenute nel capitolo. Sii specifico, non scrivere, per esempio, famiglia, ma indica se di origine o attuale; non scrivere azienda, ma indica se tutta, il tuo ufficio, il tuo team, il tuo reparto.

Associa a ogni gruppo una parola che rappresenti la direzione di emancipazione che desideri concorrere a determinare per quel gruppo. 

Impegnati con te stesso su un primo piccolo gesto che farai per iniziare quel percorso e scrivilo.

title: Esserci
content:
Se pensiamo a un momento nel quale ci siamo davvero sentiti pienamente accettati e compresi, ci rifacciamo quasi certamente a una situazione nella quale qualcuno si è relazionato con noi e ci ha ascoltati "essendoci", prendendosi cura di noi, testimoniando I così la sua totale presenza. Quando si assume questo atteggiamento, ci si propone come persone a tutto tondo, con tutti se stessi, non solo nei ruoli che si è chiamati normalmente a esercitare. Possiamo ben dire che quando accade questa magia, percepiamo tutto il benefico calore dell'autenticità.

Proprio dal distacco dei ruoli e dai comportamenti stereotipati germoglia l'autenticità. 

Scegliere questo atteggiamento presuppone il fatto di promovere altruisticamente il benessere degli altri, presuppone il saper andare oltre I propri pregiudizi e le proprie opinioni e voler condividere ciò che si sente, più ciò che si pensa.

Se rifiuti un ambiente non ti può venire possibile migliorarlo: non si migliora ciò che si rifiuta. 
Questo riguarda anche noi: il proprio miglioramento passa attraverso l'accettazione di se stessi. 

Che significato può avere l'esistenza di chi emula modelli?

Solo un progressivo allineamento con se stessi, con il proprio "centro", può favorire l'alleviamento del senso di solitudine connaturato alla natura umana e determinare relazioni più autentiche con gli altri. 
Ma non si è il proprio intimo sè solo perchè un giorno si è scelto così: il vero discrimine fra gli esseri umani, da questo punto di vista, non è fra chi è la propria autentica essenza e chi non lo è, ma fra chi sceglie di conoscerla e accettarla e chi, per paura, ne rifugge. 

La voce interiore che ci guida sulla via dell'autenticità non ci suggerisce dipendenze e false passioni che riempiano il nostro tempo, ci conduce semmai verso una nuova percezione della rinuncia come sperimentazione della nostra capacità di discernere ciò che è essenziale da ciò che non lo è.

==Pratica==
Pensa a una situazione in cui ti sei sentito accolto e compreso. Descrivi che cosa ha caratterizzato il comportamento dell'interlocutore.

title: L'arte di voler bene
content:
La benevolenza è un sentimento lineare e semplice. L'amore è invece, come le persone, multiforme e contraddittorio. 

L'amore per ciò che l'altro rappresenta e non per l'altro come persona sia destinato a produrre sofferenza. 

La dimensione benevola si fonda sul desiderio del bene dell'altro e sulla promozione della sua libertà; quando si vive questa dimensione, ci si sente legati e non vincolati ; l'altro è accettato per come è, non si desidera il suo cambiamento e anzi si vuole bene anche a quelli che potrebbero apparire come difetti.

Un individuo "risolto" (liberato: vipassana), convivente con la misteriosa ineluttabilità della morte è consapevole di come essa faccia parte della vita, può esprimere autenticamente se stesso senza sentirsi in obbligo di dichiarare guerra a qualcuno. Per questo l'uomo è un animale intrinsecamente orientato al bene e che solo l'impegno alla crescita personale può far emergere questa natura. Solo un atteggiamento benevolo consente dunque di esserci pienamente e autenticamente. 

Nel saper apprezzare ciò che si ha, indipendentemente dai propri motivi di lamentazione, c'è l'umiltà dei forti, quell'energia che ci concette di non trasformare la consapevolezza in frustrazione e ci motiva anzi ad andare oltre ogni recriminazione e a regalare al mondo I nostri talenti.

Citazione di Erri De Luca: "Considero valore il provare gratitudine senza ricordare di che"

==Pratica==
Scrivi nomi di 5 persone, presenti oggi nella tua vita, a cui vuoi bene e a cui non l'hai mai detto. Sotto l'elenco, scrivi "vi voglio bene"

Carlo
Gijsje
Samuel
Elena
Caterina

Vi voglio bene ragazzi

Pensa a una persona che potrebbe darti aiuto in qualcosa di specifico che, per pudore o orgoglio, non hai interpellato; prendi un impegno con te stesso: scrivi come e quando gli chiederai aiuto.

title: L'energia del perdono
content:
accettare pienamente se stessi presuppone la volontà di perdonarsi, o meglio, di perdonare alcune parti di sé.
La non accettazione di una parte di sè porta al tentativo della sua repressione. Ciò determina normalmente comportamenti in autentici e anche nevrotici. Inoltre, il rifiuto di una nostra parte ci impedisce di vederne gli aspetti più luminosi e talentosi. 

Il perdono è il regalo più grande che si possa fare allo stesso momento agli altri e a se stessi. 

'Sono fatto così, cosa vuoi che ci faccia' in questo caso deresponsabilizziamo noi stessi fingendo di non possedere alcuna forma di libero arbitrio e attribuendo tutta la responsabilità a un tratto della nostra personalità che non possiamo governare. 

L'atteggiamento più orientato alla nostra crescita si fonda invece, quando necessario, sulla piena coscienza dell'inadeguatezza del nostro comportamento. Quando assumiamo questo atteggiamento non cerchiamo alibi e giustificazioni e ci sentiamo invece responsabili di aver mal utilizzato una nostra caratteristica. 
Come imparare a usarla meglio in futuro? Il contrario dell'alibi non è l'autoflagellazione, ma il perdono. Perdonati per aver utilizzato male quella parte di te, per non aver ancora colto I talenti. 

Solo perdonando se stessi si possono perdonare gli altri e, non riuscendo a perdonare gli altri, non si saprà mai perdonare se stessi. 

Quando serbiamo rancore nei confronti di qualcuno, con ogni probabilità non abbiamo ancora perdonato noi stessi. Solo perdonando se stessi è possibile giungere a un autentico perdono degli altri. 

Il perdono ci libera dal peso di sentimenti in autentici. Il rancore infatti, derivando da un mancato perdono di sè, è indice di un rapporto incompiuto con se stessi.
Certo in ogni caso occorre ricordarsi della via di mezzo, della corda dell'area che deve poter suonare ma non deve spezzarsi: vera e giusta è la raccomandazione di porgere l'altra guancia; è anche vero però, che il buon "dio" o chi per lui, di guance, opportunamente, ce ne ha fornite solo due. 

==Pratica==
Pensa a una relazione irrisolta con una persona verso la quale serbi un certo rancore. 
Rivisualizza quella situazione e il tuo comportamento. Scrivi, parlando di te in terza persona, il racconto di quella situazione. 
Individua aspetti inadeguati del tuo comportamento, o meglio del comportamento di parti di te non adeguate a contesto.
Mettile in evidenza nel racconto, sempre in terza persona.

Rileggi il tuo racconto e osservati con la benevolenza con la quale un genitore guarda a un suo figliolo "difficile". Poi perdonati.
Forse, nei prossimi giorni, quell'evento si stamperà nella tua mente.

title: Autenticità e conflitto
content:
Aggregare contro è più facile che aggregare per: nel secondo caso ci vogliono anche delle idee, nel primo caso è sufficiente distruggere quelle degli altri. 

L'adozione di un comportamento autentico implica la volontà d *ascoltare il contesto* in cui ci si trova, superando la malcelata tentazione di mettersi al centro dell'attenzione per dimostrare agli altri la nostra presunta autenticità.

Ricercare lo stare insieme non significa ricercare il compromesso; esso è piuttosto l'esito di conflitti irrisolti. La ricerca dello stare insieme presuppone invece la determinazione a trovare *sintesi più altre*, rinunciando alla tentazione del conflitto a ogni costo e della ricerca di un nemico da sconfiggere. Per evitare la trappola della discora idea di autenticità occorre dunque superare la cultura dell'*essere contro* e andare verso la cultura dell'*essere per*.

Un autentico ascolto dell'altro e delle sue ragione presuppone l'assunzione, seppur provvisioria, del suo punto di vista. 

I veri innovatori si pongono a favore. A favore di un'idea espressa da altri o per promuovere una nuova idea propria. È in questo modo che si va *controcorrente*.

"Quando due bufali combattono fra loro, chi sicuramente perde è la terra che calpestano"

==Pratica==
Pensa a persona con cui hai rapporto prudente e formale. Scrivi cosa puoi fare per avvicinarti al punto di massima autneticità sostenibile. 

Pensa a cosa contro cui ti senti di combattere. Scrivila. Pensa ora a tre cose positive di questa cosa. Scrivile. Potresti non riuscirci. Non riuscirci è comunque fonte di apprendimento.

title: Questione di responsabilità
content:
Ciascuno di noi, in ogni circostanza, ha la possibilità di influire. Ogni persona, in qualsiasi contesto, è libera di scegliere quale atteggiamento assumere. 

Le nostre scelte ci formano, ci mettono alla prova e raccontano la nostra identità. 
In ogni scelta utilizziamo la nostra libertà e ce ne assumiamo inevitabilmente le conseguenti responsabilità. Nell'esercizio della nostra libertà e nell'assunzione delle conseguenti responsabilità non si esprime solo la nostra leadership ma soprattutto la nostra identità di individui. 
L'autenticità di un individuo si svela attraverso l'esercizio della sua libertà.

Sono gli individui a determinare le qualità del mondo e dalla volontà degli individui di progredire ed emanciparsi singolarmente può derivare il miglioramento della società nel suo complesso. 
Gli individui crescono maggiormente concentrandosi sulle loro aspirazioni e su ciò che può concorrere a realizzarle, piuttosto che sul miglioramento degli aspetti carenziali.
Impegnati per ciò che desideri, non per ciò che non va. 

==Pratica==
Scrivi 10 cose che devi fare (anche se non ti piacciono). Scrivile ripetendo per dieci volte la formula 'io devo...'

Ora riscrivi l'elenco sostituendo io devo con io voglio. Rileggilo a voce alta.
Ora riscrivi l'elenco sostituendo io voglio con io scelgo di. 

- Io devo smettere di fumare erba
- Io devo leggere almeno 45 minuti al giorno
- Io devo meditare almeno 45 minuti al giorno
- Io devo fare una doccia fredda al giorno
- Io devo allenarmi almeno 3/4 volte a settimana
- Io devo smetterla di concentrarmi sulle distrazioni di carlo
- Io devo essere EQUANIME e responsabile per le scelte fatte con I miei soldi
-

title: Alla ricerca della perfezione perduta
content:
Evolvere non significa dunque cambiare, ma ritrovare se stessi, la propria perfezione perduta, la propria autentica essenza, ciò che si è contaminato diventando grandi e che solo crescendo possiamo ritrovare. Vivere autenticamente presuppone scegliere di mettersi nel percorso della scoperta di sé, crescere significa dunque ritrovarsi. 

Mettiamoci dunque in cammino. Esploriamo I nostri interpreti del sé, riabilitiamo I nostri "figli difficili" interiori, scoviamo I talenti di ciascuno di loro, quei talenti che ci rappresentano, ci definiscono, ci disegnano nel mondo e nutroil nostro sé identitaria.
Potremo così ricercare la nostra perfezione perduta, crescere senza cambiare e , ritrovandosi, metterci al servizio del mondo, regalandogli la nostra più autentica a essenza. 

Potremo così moire forse ignari del nostro talento, ma, avendolo ricercato, sapremo di aver vissuto una via autentica. 

==Pratica==
Indica 3 motivi per cui puoi essere grato a te stesso.
Indica 3 motivi per cui puoi essere grato alla tua famiglia di origine.
Indica 3 motivi per cui ti senti fortunato.
Libera la voce interiore del tuo fanciullino, fa di questi nove motivi la tua preghiera laica e scrivila:
- Ringrazio me stesso per ...
- Ringrazio chi mi ha cresciuto per ...
- Ringrazio ciò che trascende la mia coscienza per ...

title: Autenticità e coerenza
content:
Un altro equivoco dell'autenticità riguarda questa distorta idea di coerenza. Sono coerente: non cambio idea e resto uguale a me stesso, o meglio a *un certo me stesso*. Ma quale coerenza garantirebbe il restare uguali a se stessi in un mondo che nel frattempo cambia? In un mondo che evolve, il nostro rapporto con esso, per restare immutato nella sua natura, deve inevitabilmente cambiare. 
Per essere coerenti dobbiamo dunque cambiare col divenire del mondo. Dal rifiuto di ciò deriva una falsa concezione che sarebbe più appropriato chiamare *nostalgia*. 

==Pratica==
Elenca 5 opinioni che hai cambiato nel corso della tua vita. Possono essere opinioni su persone, comportamenti, idee e altro. Descrivile in dettaglio.

- Apparire sui social media mi ha sempre dato senso di forza e grande ego di cui mi nutrivo
- Cate Marangoni: la persona brillante e squisita che è
- Carlo ho scoperto la sua vera natura
- Babacar Pouye e le qualità trasmesse
-

title: Esplorare il proprio talento
content:
per entrare in contatto con i propri talenti occorre assumere un atteggiamento profondamente umile. 

Diffuso è l'atteggiamento fondato sulle seguenti convinzioni:
- I miei successi derivano dai miei meriti e talenti
- I successi degli altri derivano da fattori esterni come la fortuna o le amicizie giuste
- I miei insuccessi derivano da sfortuna o incomprensione da parte degli altri. 
- Gli insuccessi degli altri sono l'inedita bile conseguenza della loro inettitudine

Questo atteggiamento deresponsabilizzante è ingegneroso innanzitutto verso se stessi: chi non sa riconoscere I talenti degli altri, non sa guardare neppure ai propri. 

Equivoco sul talento: si disvela con evidenza nel risultato. Il talento si cela nei fattori che abilitano e favoriscono l'ottenimento di un risultato. 

Per riconoscere un albero occorre osservarne I frutti: guardandoli abbiamo un immediata percezione dell'albero stesso. Allo stesso modo, per capire noi stessi è necessario partire dai nostri successi. 

È grazie all'esplorazione dei talenti dei nostri interpreti del sè che rafforziamo il nostro sé identitaria, quella parte di noi nella quale albergano I nostri talenti allo stato originale, non contaminati; quella parte di noi che, come vedremo, può dare una risposta alle nostre aspirazioni spirituali e muovere la nostra voce interiore. 

==Pratica==
Pensa a un personaggio pubblico del mondo dello spettacolo per il quale non provi alcuna stima. Ora scrivi il suo nome sul quaderno. Elenca 3 suoi talenti che hanno favorito il suo successo. Attenzione a non indicare, sia pure sotto forma di talento, I tuoi pregiudizi nei suoi confronti. 

Pensa a un personaggio pubblico del mondo della politica e fai altrettanto.

Pensa al tuo capo e scrivi il suo nome. Sospendi il tuo giudizio su di lui e guardalo con sguardo benevolo, vedilo comunque come simpatico. 
Spiega tre motivi per cui "ti è simpatico". Puoi anche indicare come ragioni di simpatia I suoi "difetti", purchè guardati e descritti con sguardo benevolo.
Ora indica 3 suoi talenti. 

Racconta adesso, utilizzando la terza persona e osservando le raccomandazioni contenute nel capitolo, tre situazioni che consideri tuoi personali successi. Ricorda: possono essere successi anche non riconosciuti o situazioni mai raccontate.

title: Gli equivoci del talento
content:
La mappa non è il territorio: il territorio è uno, ma ciascuno lo vive con la propria mappa. 

È necessario uno spostamento di paradigma: dalle persone di talento, verso i talenti delle persone. 

Non cercare persone di talento, cerca il talento nelle persone.

A volte confondiamo le nostre passioni con talenti. Non è detto che quando esprimiamo un talento, il nostro stato d'animo sia riconducibile alla gioia. Questo non accade quando si coltiva una passione: per quella c'è sempre spazio, energie disponibili, anche se non è detto che I nostri talenti corrispondano sempre alle nostre passioni. 

Le passioni vanno coltivate con la consapevolezza che a esse non necessariamente corrispondono dei talenti. 

Secondo Chelo, gli individui non si realizzano facendo ciò che più amano, ma facendo ciò in cui sono bravi. È nell'espresssione del talento che si svela l'identità di un individuo e quindi si può compiere la sua realizzazione. 

Altro equivoco sul talento: tendiamo a confondere il talento con il risultato. Il risultato non è il talento, semmai è l'effetto del talento.

La paura è la principale fonte del più grande torto che un individuo possa fare al mondo e a se stesso: rinunciare al proprio talento e sotterrarlo. Se è vero che nel talento risiede l'identità di un individuo, allora la paura è alla base dell'inautenticità.

Occorre quindi affermare una concezione di talento che sappia andare oltre il semplice obbiettivo di individuare e trattenere I cosiddetti talenti, quegli individui che possono fare la differenza, e sia invece fondata sulla fiducia nelle possibilità di tutti gli individui.
Non si tratta solo di cosa fare per trattenere le persone di talento, ma anche come favorire l'espressione dei talenti di tutte le persone. 

Ritengo insomma che occorra scegliere vie più coraggiose, meno illusorie, più efficaci. È necessario abbandonare l'ossessione della ricerca delle persone di talento per la curiosità dell'esplorazione dei talenti delle persone. Solo così potremo concorrere all'affermazione della più intima identità degli individui e quindi rendere più autentica la loro stessa esistenza e migliori I mondi che abitiamo (POESIA)

==Pratica==
Pensa alle tue passioni. C'è una passione alla quale non corrisponde un talento?
- Matematica
Scrivi un talento che ti è stato attribuito, ma nel quale non ti riconosci. 

Pensa ai tuoi talenti. Possono appartenere alla sfera della mente, del cuore o del corpo. List 3
- Determinazione 
- Introspezione
- i miei cazzo di piedi da atleta

Scrivi un tuo talento per nulla creativo
- capacità di lavorare incessantemente

title: Notes Pornland
content:
The messages that porn disseminates about women can be boiled down to a few essential characteristics: they are always ready for sex and are enthusiastic to do whatever men want, irrespective of how painful, humiliating, or harmful the act is. The word “no” is glaringly absent from porn women’s vocabulary. These women seem eager to have their orifices stretched to full capacity and sometimes beyond, and indeed, the more bizarre and degrading the act, the greater the supposed sexual arousal for her. The women who wander through this world are, whether they know it or not, all whores by nature, as they all have a price, often as low as a few bucks. 
Even though these women love to be fucked, they seem to have no sexual imagination of their own: what they want always mirrors what the man wants. This may explain why women in porn spend enormous amounts of time giving men oral sex, yet they rarely expect or demand reciprocity. Actually, the only demands they seem to make involve asking the man to thrust harder and harder.

In the porn world, women are never concerned about pregnancy, STDs, or damage to their bodies, and are astonishingly immune to being called cunts, whores, cumdumpsters, sluts, bitches, hot slits, fucktubes, squirty skanks, and stupid hoes. They seem comfortable with the idea that their partner(s) views their sexuality as something unclean (as in “dirty cunt,” “filthy little whore,” or “nasty cumdumpster”) and often refer to themselves in these ways. Indeed, women of the porn world seem to enjoy having sex with men who express nothing but contempt and hatred for them, and often the greater the insults, the better the orgasm for all involved. This is an uncomplicated world where women don’t need equal pay, health care, day care, retirement plans, good schools for their children, or safe housing. It is a world filled with one-dimensional women who are nothing more than collections of holes.

The story pornography tells about men is actually much simpler. Men in porn are depicted as soulless, unfeeling, amoral life-support systems for erect penises who are entitled to use women in any way they want. These men demonstrate zero empathy, respect, or love for the women they have sex with, no matter how uncomfortable or in pain these woman look. Probably the most peculiar thing about porn-world men is that they even lack the ability to express arousal, because no matter how erect their penises are, they exhibit none of the signs we normally associate with sexual excitement. The only time the men moan, grunt, or writhe is when they are about to ejaculate; the rest of the time they methodically thrust their penis into the woman’s orifices with a look of deep concentration on their faces. This can get very bizarre, especially in an oral sex scene where a stoic man gags a woman by thrusting his penis deep in her mouth, yet she is the only one having orgasmic responses. And when porn men are done, they are really done—there is not the slightest show of postcoital intimacy with the woman they have just ejaculated onto.

One way to think about effects is to turn the question around; rather than asking how porn affects users, we could ask, Under what conditions would the images in porn not have an effect? In other words, what do men need to be exposed to in order to counter the stories in porn?

Today, a girl or young woman looking for an alternative to the Britney, Paris, Lindsay look will soon come to the grim realization that the only alternative to looking fuckable is to be invisible.

What resistance to porn offers men is a sexuality that celebrates connectedness, intimacy, and empathy—a sexuality bathed in equality rather than subordination.

title: Webu Sayadaw
content:
When the air comes in, it will touch. When the air goes out, it will touch. If you know this touch continuously, then wanting (lobha), dislike (dosa), and delusion (moha) do not have the opportunity to arise, and the fires of greed, anger, and delusion will subside.

You cannot know the touch of air before it actually occurs. After it has gone, you cannot know it any more. Only while the air moves in or out can you feel the sensation of touch. This we call the present moment.

Don't let go of the sensation produced by the breath touching the skin. Remain with the awareness of touch. Don't follow the stream of air inside or outside. And why? If you do that, you won't be able to feel the touch sensation. So, let's stay with the awareness of the spot without a break. 
D: Do we have to be aware of the touch of air in both nostrils or just in one? 
S: Feel only one. If you try to feel two places your attention will be split. Put your undivided attention on one spot.

If you know for yourself whether you have got some understanding or not, then you are on the right path. If you know when you don't understand, you have understood. But if you think you have understood though you haven't understood a thing, then there is not the slightest hope for you to acquire any understanding.

the eight constituents of the Noble Eightfold Path are: 
(1) right view, 
(2) right thought, 
(3) right speech, 
(4) right action, 
(5) right livelihood, 
(6) right effort, 
(7) right mindfulness, 
(8) right concentration. 
When our mind is fixed on the spot, we don't think unskilful thoughts in any way.

In this way happiness and bliss come to you. When one has attained this happiness, when one has received the sign of bliss, when one has reached the goal, there is no jealous guarding of a secret. No, you will want all human beings, devas, and brahmas to attain this bliss and happiness. As you know for yourself how to attain this happiness, the actions of body, speech, and mind will always be in harmony with the cause of attaining Nibbana. You will act with joy as your base.

The Buddha said that this is the highest form of patience: to forbear even though you do not have to, even though you could change the situation. Of course it is also good to practise forbearance when you have no other choice, but to forbear voluntarily is the highest and best sort of forbearance.

awareness means that the meditator takes note of the in-breath as it touches the nostrils and of the out-breath as it touches the nostrils. As breathing is continuous, so awareness must be continuous too. Only then can awareness be properly called vipassana meditation.

Appearance and disappearance of vibrating manifestations are the process of becoming and cessation. 14. As meditators notice the swiftly changing process of appearance and disappearance of contact sensations at the nostrils, concentrated insight {vipassana-samadhi) develops in due course, that is to say, after a considerable length of time. The concentration developed in this way becomes more and more intense until a meditator becomes aware of swiftly sweeping changes all over the body. 15. When these swiftly sweeping changes are seen with insight, the characteristic of anicca becomes most obvious, and accordingly the characteristics of dukkha and anattd are also seen. It is not necessary to utter them by word of mouth. Vipassana meditation means being mindful of what actually happens. Mindfulness develops day by day, and consequently, meditators gain penetrating insight.
file_path: Spiritual/The Way to Ultimate Calm/Notes - Webu Sayadaw.md

title: The warrior is free. But he knows that an open oven bakes no bread.
content:
The warrior hears someone say: 'I need to understand everything before I can make a decision. I want to have the freedom to change my mind.'

The warrior regards these words suspiciously. He too can enjoy that freedom, but this does not prevent him from taking on a commitment, even if he does not know quite why he does so.

A warrior of light makes decisions. His soul is as free as the clouds in the sky, but he is committed to his dream. On his freely chosen path, he often has to get up earlier than he would like, speak to people from whom he learns nothing, make certain sacrifices.

His friends say: 'You're not free.'
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior is free. But he knows that an open oven bakes no bread..md

title: Warriors of light always have a certain gleam in their eyes.
content:
They are of this world, they are part of the lives of other people and they set out on their journey with no saddlebags and no sandals. They are often cowardly. They do not always make the right decisions.

They suffer over the most trivial things, they have mean thoughts and sometimes believe they are incapable of growing. They frequently deem themselves unworthy of any blessing or miracle.

They are not always quite sure what they are doing here.

They spend many sleepless nights, believing that their lives have no meaning.

That is why they are warriors of light. Because they make mistakes. Because they ask themselves questions. Because they are looking for a reason - and are sure to find it.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/Warriors of light always have a certain gleam in their eyes..md

title: The warrior of light knows that no one is stupid and that life teaches everyone - however long that may take.
content:
He always does his best and expects the best of others.

Through his generosity, he tries to show each person how much they are capable of achieving.

Some of his companions say: 'Some people are so ungrateful.' The warrior is not discouraged by this. And he continues to encourage other people because this is also a way of encouraging himself.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light knows that no one is stupid and that life teaches everyone - however long that may take..md

title: A warrior of light does not rely on strength alone, he makes use of his opponent's energy too.
content:
When he enters the fight, all he has is his enthusiasm and the moves and strikes that he learned during his training. As the fight progresses, he discovers that enthusiasm and training are not enough to win: what counts is experience.
Then he opens his heart to the Universe and asks God to give him the inspiration he needs to turn every blow from his enemy into a lesson in self-defence.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light does not rely on strength alone, he makes use of his opponent's energy too..md

title: A warrior of light sometimes thinks 'If I do not do something, it will not be done.'
content: It is not quite like that: he must act, but he must allow room for the Universe to act too.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light sometimes thinks 'If I do not do something, it will not be done.'.md

title: The warrior of light always manages to balance Rigour and Mercy.
content:
To attain his dream, he needs a strong will and an enormous capacity for acceptance; although he may have an objective, the path that leads to that objective is not always as he imagined it would be.

That is why the warrior uses a mixture of discipline and compassion. God never abandons His children, but His purposes are unfathomable, and He builds the road with our own steps.

The warrior uses that combination of discipline and acceptance to fuel his enthusiasm. Routine was never at the head to obtain important new movement.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light always manages to balance Rigour and Mercy..md

title: A warrior of light never resorts to trickery, but he knows how to distract his opponent.
content:
However anxious he is, he uses every strategy at his disposal to gain his objective. When he sees that his strength is almost gone, he makes his enemy think that he is simply biding his time. When he needs to attack the right flank, he moves his troops to the left. If he intends beginning the battle at once, he pretends that he is tired and prepares for sleep.
A warrior of light knows what he wants. And he has no need to waste time on explanations.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light never resorts to trickery, but he knows how to distract his opponent..md

title: A warrior of light carefully studies the position that he intends to conquer.
content:
However difficult the objective, there is always a way of overcoming obstacles. He seeks out alternative paths, he sharpens his sword, he tries to fill his heart with the necessary determination to face the challenge.

But as he advances, the warrior realises that there are difficulties he had not reckoned with.

If he waits for the ideal moment, he will never set off; he requires a touch of madness to take the next step.

The warrior uses that touch of madness. For - in both love and war - it is impossible to foresee everything.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light carefully studies the position that he intends to conquer..md

title: A warrior balances solitude and dependence.
content:
He usually tries to be alone, in order not to show his pain to others.

This is both good and bad.

It is one thing to allow one's heart to heal its wounds slowly, but it is quite another to sit all day in deep contemplation for fear of seeming weak.

Inside each of us there lives an angel and a devil, and their voices are very alike. Confronted by a problem, the devil encourages that solitary conversation, trying to show us how vulnerable we are. 
The angel makes us reflect upon our attitudes and occasionally needs someone else's mouth to reveal itself.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior balances solitude and dependence..md

title: 'Convince your enemy that he will gain very little by attacking you; this will diminish his enthusiasm.
content:
'Do not be ashamed to make a temporary withdrawal from the field if you see that your enemy is stronger than you; it is not winning or losing a single battle that matters, but how the war ends.'
'Even if you are very strong, never be ashamed to feign weakness; this will make your enemy act imprudently and attack too soon.'
In war, the key to victory is the ability to surprise one's opponent.'
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/'Convince your enemy that he will gain very little by attacking you; this will diminish his enthusiasm..md

title: The warrior of light meditates.
content:
He sits in a quiet place in his tent and surrenders himself to the divine light. When he does this, he tries not to think about anything; he shuts himself off from the search for pleasure, from challenges and revelations, and allows his gifts and powers to reveal themselves.

Even if he does not recognise them then, these gifts and powers are taking care of his life and will influence his day-to-day existence.

While he meditates, the warrior is not himself, but a spark from the Soul of the World. These are the moments that give him an understanding of his responsibilities and of how he should behave accordingly.

A warrior of light knows that in the silence of his heart he will hear an order that will guide him.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light meditates..md

title: The warrior of light is never cowardly.
content:
Flight might be an excellent form of defence, but it cannot be used when one is very afraid. When in doubt, the warrior prefers to face defeat and then lick his wounds, because he knows that if he flees he is giving to the aggressor greater power than he deserves.

In difficult and painful times, the warrior faces overwhelming odds with heroism, resignation and courage.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light is never cowardly..md

title: A warrior of light is never predictable.
content:
He might dance down the street on his way to work, gaze into the eyes of a complete stranger and speak of love at first sight, or defend an apparently absurd idea. Warriors of light allow themselves days like these.

He is not afraid to weep over ancient sorrows or feel joy at new discoveries. When he feels that the moment has arrived, he drops everything and goes off on some long dreamed of adventure. When he realises that he can do no more, he abandons the fight, but never blames himself for having committed a few unexpected acts of folly.

A warrior does not spend his days trying to play the role that others have chosen for him.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light is never predictable..md

title: The warrior of light sometimes fights with those he loves.
content:
The man who defends his friends is never overwhelmed by the storms of life; he is strong enough to come through difficulties and to carry on.
However, he is often faced by challenges from those he is trying to teach the art of the sword. His disciples provoke him into fighting with them.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light sometimes fights with those he loves..md

title: The warrior of light is never in a hurry.
content:
Time works in his favour; he learns to master his impatience and avoids acting without thinking.

By walking slowly, he becomes aware of the firmness of his step. He knows that he is taking part in a decisive moment in the history of humanity and that he needs to change himself before he can transform the world. That is why he remembers the words of Lanza del Vasto: 'A revolution takes time to settle in.'

A warrior never picks fruit while it is still green.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light is never in a hurry..md

title: A warrior of light is in the world in order to help his fellow man, not to condemn his neighbour.
content:
The warrior of light is never indifferent to injustice.

He knows that all is one and that each individual action affects everyone on the planet. That is why, when confronted by the suffering of others, he uses his sword to restore order.

But even though he fights against oppression, at no point does he attempt to judge the oppressor. Each person will answer for his actions before God and so, once the warrior has completed his task, he makes no further comment.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light is in the world in order to help his fellow man, not to condemn his neighbour..md

title: The moment that he begins to walk along it, the warrior of light recognises the Path.
content:
He identifies with the mountains and the streams, he sees something of his own soul in the plants and the animals and the birds of the field.

Then, accepting the help of God and of God's Signs, he allows his Personal Legend to guide him towards the tasks that life has reserved for him.

On some nights, he has nowhere to sleep, on others, he suffers from insomnia. 'That's just how it is,' thinks the warrior.

I was the one who chose to walk this path.'

In these words lies all his power: he chose the path along which he is walking and so has no complaints.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The moment that he begins to walk along it, the warrior of light recognises the Path..md

title: The warrior of light knows that it is impossible to live in a state of complete relaxation.
content:
He has learned from the archer that, in order to shoot his arrow any distance, he must hold the bow taut. He has learned from the stars that only an inner explosion allows them to shine. The warrior notices that when a horse is about to jump over a fence, it tenses all its muscles.

But he never confuses tension with anxiety.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light knows that it is impossible to live in a state of complete relaxation..md

title: The warrior remembers the past.
content:
He knows about man's Spiritual Quest, he knows that this Quest has been responsible for some of history's finest pages.

But also some of history's worst chapters: massacres, sacrifices, obscurantism. It was used for personal ends and has seen its ideas used to defend the most terrible of intentions.

The warrior has heard people ask: 'How am I to know that the path I am on is the right path?' And he has seen many people abandon their quest because they could not answer that question.

The warrior has no doubts; he follows one infallible saying:

'By their fruits ye shall know them, said Jesus. That is the rule he follows, and he never goes wrong.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior remembers the past..md

title: The warrior of light often loses heart.
content:
He believes that nothing can stir in him the emotion he so desired

He is forced to spend many evenings and nights feeling that he is one of the vanquished, and nothing seems able to restore his enthusiasm.

His friends say: 'Perhaps his fight is over.'

The warrior feels pain and confusion when he hears such remarks because he knows that he has not yet reached the place he wanted to reach. But he is stubborn and refuses to relinquish his aims.

Then, when he least expects it, a new door opens.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light often loses heart..md

title: 'The warrior of light chooses his enemies.'
content:
He knows what he is capable of; he does not have to go about the world boasting of his qualities and virtues.

Nevertheless, there is always someone who wants to prove himself better than he is.

For the warrior, there is no 'better' or 'worse': everyone has the necessary gifts for his particular path.

But certain people insist. They provoke and offend and do everything they can to irritate him. At that point, his heart says: 'Do not respond to these insults, they will not increase your abilities. You will tire yourself needlessly!

A warrior of light does not waste his time listening to provocations; he has a destiny to fulfil.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/'The warrior of light chooses his enemies.'.md

title: The warrior knows that his best teachers are the people with whom he shares the battlefield.
content:
It is dangerous to ask for advice. It is even more dangerous to give advice. When he needs help, he tries to see how his friends resolve - or fail to resolve - their problems.

If he is in search of inspiration, he reads on the lips of his neighbour the words that his guardian angel is trying to say to him.

When he is tired or lonely, he does not dream about distant men and women; he turns to the person beside him and shares his sorrow or his need for affection with them - with pleasure and without guilt.

A warrior knows that the farthest-flung star in the Universe reveals itself in the things around him.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior knows that his best teachers are the people with whom he shares the battlefield..md

title: Before embarking on an important battle, a warrior of light asks himself ‘how far have I developed my abilities?'
content: Victors never make the same mistake twice. That is why the warrior only risks his heart for something worthwhile.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/Before embarking on an important battle, a warrior of light asks himself ‘how far have I developed my abilities?'.md

title: The warrior of light sometimes behaves like water, flowing around the obstacles he encounters.
content:
Occasionally, resisting might mean being destroyed, and so he adapts to the circumstances. He accepts without complaint that the stones along the path hinder his way across the mountains.

Therein lies the strength of water: it cannot be shattered by a hammer or wounded by a knife. The strongest sword in the world cannot scar its surface.

The waters of a river adapt themselves to whatever route proves possible, but the river never forgets its one objective: the sea. So fragile at its source, it gradually gathers the strength of the other rivers it encounters.

And, after a certain point, its power is absolute.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/The warrior of light sometimes behaves like water, flowing around the obstacles he encounters..md

title: A warrior of light knows that certain moments repeat themselves.
content:
He often finds himself faced by the same problems and situations, and seeing these difficult situations return, he grows depressed, thinking that he is incapable of making any progress in life.

I've been through all this before,' he says to his heart.
"Yes, you have been through all this before, replies his heart.
'But you have never been beyond it.'

Then the warrior realises that these repeated experiences have but one aim: **to teach him what he does not want to learn.**
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light knows that certain moments repeat themselves..md

title: A warrior of light thinks about both war and peace and knows how to act in accordance with the circumstances.
content:
The warrior of light has the qualities of a rock.

When he is on flat terrain, everything around him is in harmony and he remains stable. People can build their houses upon him, and the storm will not destroy them.

When, however, he is placed on a slope, and the things around him show neither balance nor respect, then he reveals his strength; he rolls towards the enemy that is threatening his peace. At such moments, the warrior is a devastating force, and no one can stop him.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light thinks about both war and peace and knows how to act in accordance with the circumstances..md

title: In order to have faith in his own path, he does not need to prove that someone else's path is wrong.
content: Anything of importance will remain; anything useless will disappear. However, it is not the warrior's responsibility to judge the dreams of others, and he does not waste time criticising other people's decisions.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/In order to have faith in his own path, he does not need to prove that someone else's path is wrong..md

title: A warrior can distinguish between the transient and the enduring.
content:
All the world's roads lead to the heart of the warrior; he plunges unhesitatingly into the river of passions always flowing through his life.
The warrior knows that he is free to choose his desires, and he makes these decisions with courage, detachment and - sometimes - with just a touch of madness.
He embraces his passions and enjoys them intensely. He knows that there is no need to renounce the pleasures of conquest; they are part of life and bring joy to all those who participate in them.
But he never loses sight of those things that last or of the strong bonds that are forged over time.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior can distinguish between the transient and the enduring..md

title: A warrior of light respects the main teaching of the I Ching - 'To persevere is favourable.'
content:
He knows that perseverance is not the same thing as insistence. There are times when battles go on longer than necessary, draining him of strength and enthusiasm.

At such moments, the warrior thinks: 'A prolonged war finally destroys the victors too.'
Then he withdraws his forces from the battlefield and allows himself a respite. He perseveres in his desire, but knows he must wait for the best moment to attack.

A warrior always returns to the fray. He never does so out of stubbornness, but because he has noticed a change in the weather.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light respects the main teaching of the I Ching - 'To persevere is favourable.'.md

title: 'Although I have been through all that I have, I do not regret the many hardships I met, because it was they who brought me to the place I wished to reach.
content: Now all I have is this sword and I give it to whomever wishes to continue his pilgrimage. I carry with me the marks and scars of battles - they are the witnesses of what I suffered and the rewards of what I conquered.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/'Although I have been through all that I have, I do not regret the many hardships I met, because it was they who brought me to the place I wished to reach..md

title: A warrior of light knows that he has much to be grateful for.
content: A warrior does not need to be reminded of the help given him by others; he is the first to remember and he makes sure to share with them any rewards he receives.
book: Manual of the Warrior of Light
file_path: Spiritual/Manual of the Warrior of Light/files/A warrior of light knows that he has much to be grateful for..md

title: The ego's need to feel superior
content: Remember: The moment you become aware of the ego in yourself, that emerging awareness is who you are beyond ego, the deeper “I.” The recognition of the false is already the arising of the real.
file_path: Spiritual/A New Earth/files/The ego's need to feel superior.md

title: The illusory self
content:
The word “I” embodies the greatest error and the deepest truth, depending on how it is used. In conventional usage, it is not only one of the most frequently used words in the language (together with the related words: “me, ” “my, ” “mine, ” and “myself”) but also one of the most misleading. In normal everyday usage, “I” embodies the primordial error, a misperception of who you are, an illusory sense of identity. This is the ego.

In the seeing of who you are not, the reality of who you are emerges by itself.
file_path: Spiritual/A New Earth/files/The illusory self.md

title: Content and structure of the ego
content:
The egoic mind is completely conditioned by the past. 
Its conditioning is twofold: It consists of content and structure. 

In the case of a child who cries in deep suffering because his toy has been taken away, the toy represents content. It is interchangeable with any other content, any other toy or object. 

The content you identify with is conditioned by your environment, your upbringing, and surrounding culture. 

Whether the child is rich or poor, whether the toy is a piece of wood shaped like an animal or a sophisticated electronic gadget makes no difference as far as the suffering caused by its loss is concerned. The reason why such acute suffering occurs is concealed in the word “my,” and it is structural. 

The unconscious compulsion to enhance one’s identity through association with an object is built into the very structure of the egoic mind. One of the most basic mind structures through which the ego comes into existence is identification.

So when I identify with something, I “make it the same.” The same as what? The same as I.
I endow it with a sense of self, and so it becomes part of my “identity.”

I try to find myself in things but never quite make it and end up losing myself in them. That is the fate of the ego.
file_path: Spiritual/A New Earth/files/Content and structure of the ego.md

title: Chapter 8 - The Discovery of Inner Space
content:
When you are no longer totally identified with forms, consciousness – who you are becomes freed from its imprisonment in form. This freedom is the arising of inner space. It comes as a stillness, a subtle peace deep within you, even in the face of something seemingly bad.

The words This, too, will pass are pointers toward reality. In pointing to the impermanence of all forms, by implication, they are also pointing to the eternal. Only the eternal in you can recognize the impermanent as impermanent.
